# The Feedback Loop - Continuous Improvement # **The Feedback Loop** - Continuous Improvement ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** A sales process isn’t something you “build and forget.” It breaks silently. Slowly. In the background. By the time you notice? You’ve already lost deals you should’ve closed. This page shows you how to catch issues early, diagnose and fix.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Spot breakdowns in your sales system using data ✅ Diagnose what part of your process is actually broken ✅ Apply targeted fixes using the right data from your Pipeline Tracker ✅ Build a simple weekly feedback loop to improve performance ## Why Most Founders Ignore This (and Pay For It) --- Most agency owners set up a few systems…then just hope it all works. They don’t track numbers. They don’t review weekly. They don’t fix issues until something explodes.
If your booked calls are down this week… You should know **why** That’s what this loop is for.
--- ## The Feedback Loop That Keeps Your Sales Process Working --- Every week, you should check:
✅ **Lead → Call Conversion Rate** ✅ **Call Show-Up Rate** ✅ **Call → Close Rate** ✅ **Sales Cycle Length** ✅ **Deal Drop-Off Points** (tracked in your Pipeline Tracker)
These are the symptoms. If one drops. Your job is to diagnose the cause, not just “work harder”. --- ## Here’s What Each Symptom Tells You: --- ### **1. Low Lead → Call Conversion Rate** ---
**Likely Problems:** - Weak angles or messaging - Poor qualification (wrong ICP) - Inconsistent outreach or poor follow-up **Fixes to Try:** - Rebuild campaign angles based on GTM - Tighten qualification criteria - Add follow-up automation
--- ### **2. Low Call Show-Up Rate** ---
**Likely Problems:** - Weak pre-call reminders - No show-up mechanism or Slack automation - Poor call framing (they didn’t value the meeting) **Fixes to Try:** - Install Slack reply alert zap + 15-min reply rule - Improve pre-call reminder system (email + calendar + confirmation) - Use “show-up stack” you learned in Call Prep section
--- ### **3. Low Call → Close Rate** ---
**Likely Problems:** - Weak qualification (bad-fit leads on calls) - Poor offer positioning or value articulation - Mishandled objections - No structured sales call flow **Fixes to Try:** - Revisit call recordings → where do you lose control? - Go back through $30K Call Framework + objection flow - Check were the drop off is and implement
--- ### **4. Long Sales Cycles** ---
**Likely Problems:** - No urgency triggers - Vague follow-up process - Letting “think about it” leads drift **Fixes to Try:** - Use the CTA + Reminder Stack to lock clear next steps - Maybe you need an ‘urgency offer’ (instead of taking 2 weeks we will launch in 48hours) - Install follow-up workflows in your sales process - Book the next call on the first call (Calendar Close)
--- ### **5. Dead Deals Sitting in your tracker** ---
**Likely Problems:** - No re-engagement system - Leads dumped after “no for now” - No campaign built for old deals **Fixes to Try:** - Launch a Re-Engagement Campaign from your Pipeline Tracker - Set calendar reminders for check-ins on “not now” prospects - Add cold leads into a new nurture sequence
--- ## Use Your Pipeline Tracker To Power the Loop --- You already built a Pipeline Tracker. Here’s how to make it work *for you*:
✅ Filter by status to check drop-offs ✅ Track dates between stages to see sales cycle ✅ Use “Last Contacted” to find stale leads ✅ Count “Proposals Sent” vs “Closed Won” ✅ Track which campaigns/angles lead to real revenue
## Your Weekly Feedback Loop --- Do this every week. 30 minutes max. Founder work. --- ### **1. Review Funnel Metrics** ---
- Leads → Calls - Calls → Shows - Shows → Closes - Cycle Length
### **2. Identify Drop-Off** ---
- Which metric dropped? - What changed since last week?
### **3. Isolate the Cause** ---
- Review your process for that stage - Listen to call recordings - Check what’s been skipped, missed, or outdated
### **4. Apply a Fix** ---
- Change 1 step. Test 1 variable. - Update workflow, add automation, fix follow-up
### **5. Document What You Did** ---
- Note the change in your Sales Process doc - Track result next week
## Final Reminder --- You don’t need to guess. You just need: - A clear process - A working tracker - A weekly feedback loop That’s how you build a real sales engine. If you can’t identify and fix problems in your sales process, you will never grow. ← Previous [Map Out Your Sales Process](/resources/map-out-your-sales-process-24af6d94771f80e88c0bfd08b7de7b71) Next → [Build Your Sales Process](/resources/build-your-sales-process-245f6d94771f80dc93c1ca33f9546ae1) On this page

# What A Sales Process Is + Why You Need One # **What A Sales Process Is + Why You Need One** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Agencies that document their sales process close 3x more deals than those that wing it. The difference? One relies on luck, the other relies on systems.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Understand what a sales process actually is (the full customer journey) ✅ Know why successful agencies document their entire workflow ✅ See the difference between random tactics and systematic conversion ✅ Be ready to map your complete lead-to-customer system ## What Typical Founders Get Wrong --- Most agency founders think having a "sales process" means they have a call script.
They focus only on the 30-minute call while ignoring: - How leads flow through their system - What happens between initial contact and the call - How they follow up after calls - When and how they nurture prospects who aren't ready - What steps convert leads into booked calls - How they track prospects through each stage
**The result?** Leads fall through cracks. Follow-up is inconsistent. They can't identify where prospects drop off or why some months are better than others. **The reality:** A sales process isn't just about calls - it's about the entire journey from stranger to paying customer. --- ## What A Sales Process Actually Is --- A sales process is **a structured, step-by-step approach that guides potential customers from initial contact to completed sale**.
It's your complete roadmap covering: ✅ **How leads enter your system** (marketing, referrals, outbound) ✅ **How you qualify and prioritise them** (scoring, research, filtering) ✅ **How you move them toward a call** (email sequences, follow-up, nurturing) ✅ **How you conduct the sales conversation** (the framework you learned) ✅ **How you handle different outcomes** (follow-up, objections, decisions) ✅ **How you onboard new clients** (contracts, payments, delivery)
### Your Complete Sales Process Includes: --- **1. Lead Generation & Capture**
- Inbound leads from website/content - Outbound prospecting campaigns - Referral systems - Lead qualification criteria
**2. Lead Nurturing & Qualification**
- Initial response sequences - Research and prioritisation - Booking process and requirements - Pre-call preparation and send-ahead materials
**3. Sales Conversations**
- Your call framework (what you just learned) - Follow-up sequences for different outcomes - Objection handling workflows - Decision timelines and next steps
**4. Closing & Onboarding**
- Contract and payment processes - Client onboarding workflow - Delivery handoff systems - Retention and upsell processes
## Sales Process vs. Sales Calls --- **Just Having Sales Calls:** "When someone books a call, I try to close them" **Having a Sales Process:** "Leads come in through these 3 channels, get scored using these criteria, enter this nurture sequence, book calls through this system, go through this framework during calls, receive these follow-up sequences based on outcomes, and convert into clients through this documented workflow."
ℹ️ You will see all the components you’ve learned so far, contribute to a great sales process
--- ## Why You Need A Complete Sales Process --- ### 1. Nothing Falls Through the Cracks
Right now, how many potential clients have you lost because: - You forgot to follow up? - You didn't have a system for nurturing not-ready prospects? - You couldn't track where leads came from or what stage they're in? - You had no clear next steps after calls?
A documented process makes sure every lead gets the attention they deserve. --- ### 2. You Can Identify What's Actually Broken ---
Without seeing the full process, you can't diagnose problems: - Low show-up rates? Could be your booking process, not your calls - Good calls but no closes? Could be your qualification, not your pitch - Feast or famine revenue? Could be your lead flow, not your closing
### 3. You Can Scale Beyond Yourself --- Want to hire someone to help with sales? What exactly would you hand them?
A complete sales process means you can delegate: - Lead qualification to a VA - Initial outreach to an SDR - Follow-up sequences to automation - Parts of the sales process to junior team members
### 4. You Stop Losing Winnable Deals --- Every undefined step is costing you revenue.
How many deals have you lost because: - Prospects went cold between inquiry and call? - You had no system for following up with "maybe later" prospects? - You couldn't track which marketing efforts actually generate revenue? - You had no process for handling common objections or scenarios?
### 5. You Build Predictable Revenue --- Businesses with documented sales processes can forecast revenue because they know:
- How many leads they need to generate X revenue - Which sources produce the highest-quality prospects - What their conversion rates are at each stage - Where bottlenecks occur and how to fix them
--- ## The Cost of Not Having a Complete Process --- **Agency A:** Gets leads randomly, wings follow-up, hopes calls go well, can't explain revenue fluctuations. **Agency B:** Has documented workflows for every stage, tracks conversion metrics, optimizes based on data.
After 12 months: - Agency A is still stressed about where next month's revenue will come from - Agency B has predictable lead flow, consistent conversion rates, and a scalable system **The difference?** Agency B treats their entire sales operation as a system, not a collection of random activities.
--- ## What Comes Next --- Now that you understand what a sales process is and what it does, it's time to map yours out. ← Previous [Build Your Sales Process](/resources/build-your-sales-process-245f6d94771f80dc93c1ca33f9546ae1) Next → [Map Out Your Sales Process](/resources/map-out-your-sales-process-24af6d94771f80e88c0bfd08b7de7b71) On this page

# Call Prep - Set Yourself Up to Win # Call Prep - Set Yourself Up to Win ---
**🚨QUICK NOTE:** The difference between closing 10% and 40%+ of calls often comes down to the 15 minutes before you start the call. **NOTE: This again uses Lead Generation as an example to demonstrate. Please amend to suit your service.**
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Know exactly how to research prospects in under 10 minutes ✅ Have all the numbers and materials ready to close confidently ✅ Feel mentally prepared and in control before every call ✅ Never wing another sales call again ## What Most People Get Wrong --- Most agency owners jump on calls hoping for the best instead of preparing to win. They wing the research, scramble for case studies mid-call, and wonder why prospects don't take them seriously. **The result?** Awkward pauses, weak positioning, and lost deals. **The truth:** 15 minutes of prep can double your close rate. --- ## The 4-Stage Pre-Call Prep System --- ### 1. Research Their Business (5-7 minutes) --- **What You're Looking For:**
- Their likely target market and ICP - Services they offer (to understand their expertise level) - Case studies or results on their website - Company size and maturity indicators - Any recent news, funding, or growth signals
**Quick Research Checklist:**
- [ ] Visit their website - note their main services - [ ] Check their "About" or "Case Studies" page for client types - [ ] Look at their LinkedIn for company size and recent posts - [ ] Note any specific industries they mention targeting - [ ] Identify if they're B2B or B2C focused **Tip:** Don't over-research. 7 minutes max. You'll learn more on the call through proper discovery.
### 2. Know Your Numbers Cold --- **Have These Ready:**
- [ ] Your guarantee/core offer details - [ ] 2-3 relevant case studies with specific numbers - [ ] Your value stack breakdown with market values - [ ] Typical close rates for their industry (for ROI calculations) - [ ] Your pricing structure and payment terms
### 3. Technical Preparation --- **Have These Ready to Go:**
- [ ] Agreement/contract prepared and ready to send - [ ] Payment links or invoice templates ready - [ ] Calendar for immediate follow-up scheduling - [ ] Value stack document or breakdown accessible - [ ] Any relevant case study documents or testimonials
**Quick Access Setup:**
- Bookmark your calendar scheduling link - Have agreement template saved with their name pre-filled - Keep case studies in a folder for quick access - Save your value stack as a quick-reference document
### 4. Mental Preparation & Confidence --- **Remember These Principles:**
✅ **This is a conversation, not a presentation** - Stay flexible but follow the structure ✅ **You're the expert** - They need help ✅ **Never skip discovery** - No anchor = no leverage ✅ **Always set next steps** - Control the follow-up process ✅ **Use their numbers** - Make it about them, not you ✅ **Be prepared to walk away** - Desperation kills deals
**Your Mindset Going In:***"I'm here to help them solve a real problem. If I can't help them, I'll tell them honestly and refer them to someone who can or tell them so. My job is to understand their situation and determine if we're a fit."* --- ## Final Pre-Call Checklist --- **2 Minutes Before The Call:**
- [ ] Deep breath - you're prepared - [ ] Review their company name and your research notes - [ ] Have your case studies accessible - [ ] Clear your desktop of distractions - [ ] Remember: you're here to help, not to convince
**Your Goal:** Help them solve their problem. If you can't, refer them to someone who can. --- ## Common Preparation Mistakes to Avoid ---
❌ **Over-researching** - Don't spend 30 minutes researching. You'll learn more through discovery ❌ **Under-preparing** - Don't wing it completely. 15 minutes of prep beats hoping for the best ❌ **Forgetting case studies** - Have 2-3 relevant examples ready with specific numbers ❌ **No technical setup** - Scrambling for agreements mid-call kills momentum ❌ **Wrong mindset** - You're not begging for business, you're evaluating fit
--- **Remember:** Preparation breeds confidence. Confidence closes deals. The prospect can sense when you're prepared versus when you're winging it. Show up ready, stay in control, and help them solve their problem. ← Previous [Set The Frame + Own The Call](/resources/set-the-frame-own-the-call-24af6d94771f8068a9bee7932561182d) Next → [Pre-Call](/resources/pre-call-control-the-call-before-it-starts-245f6d94771f80ea8674e9a84a5d9734) On this page

# Call-Show Up Process # Call-Show Up Process ---
**🚨QUICK NOTE:** This simple pre-call process will get you **80%+ show-up rate consistently.**
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Set up automated Calendly reminders that actually work ✅ Edit the default templates to sound like you (not a robot) ✅ Handle cancellations automatically without chasing ✅ Never wonder "will they show up?" again ## The Proven Call Show-Up Process (Use This Every Time) ---
**⚠️ You should be manually booking your calls, not using links.** So this process assumes you're inputting call info into Calendly yourself, after manually confirming times with prospects.
--- ### Overview | When | Channel | What It Sends | Why It Matters | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Immediately | Email | Confirmation + Zoom + proof + agenda | Sets tone, primes trust | | 24h Before | Email | Reminder + re-confirmation | Reduces no-shows by 10% | | 1h Before | Email | Reminder + restate value | Rebuilds desire to attend | | 10m Before | SMS | Short reminder + Zoom ref | (optional) | | On cancel | Email | “Reschedule your call” + link | Saves pipeline without chasing | ## 1. Set Up Your Calendly Event Properly --- **Open Calendly → Events → Find your sales call event → Click "Edit"** **Under "When can people book this event?"**
- Duration: 30 minutes (or whatever your call length is) - Availability: Consider offering 10am-4pm slots (highest show-up window) - Date range: Don't let them book more than 7 days out
**Why:** Mid-day calls have 25% higher show-up rates and prospects who want to speak further than 7 days out aren’t as interested as we want them to be. ## 2. Edit Your Reminder Emails (Don’t skip this) --- **Navigate to: Event Settings → Notifications & Workflows → Email Reminders**
You'll see Calendly's default reminder templates. **Don't use them as-is.** Here's how to edit them: **Click "Edit" on each reminder and customise:**
### Immediate Confirmation Email --- ``` Hey {invitee_first_name}, You're all set for {event_date} at {event_time}. Here's our [Pre-Call Guide] this will [explain what it will go over and the outcome as to why they should read it before the call] Looking forward to speaking with you. Talk soon, [Your Name] ``` ### 24-Hour Before Email --- ``` Hi {invitee_first_name}, Looking forward to speaking with you tomorrow at {event_time}. Reminder, here's our [Pre-call guide + why they should review before call]. If something's come up, just hit reply and we'll find a better time. Looking forward to it. [Your Name] ``` ### 1-Hour Before Email --- ``` Hi {invitee_first_name}, In 1 hour we'll walk through: [bullet points of how you will help/what you will share]. Zoom link: {location} See you at {event_time}, [Your Name] ``` **Calendly Placeholders You Can Use:** - {invitee_first_name} - {invitee_full_name} - {event_name} - {event_date} - {event_time} - {location} ## 3. Add SMS Reminders (Optional but helps) --- **To use SMS:** You need their phone number when you manually book the call. **In Calendly:** Notifications & Workflows → SMS Reminders → Add 10-min before reminder **SMS Template:** ``` Hey {invitee_first_name}, reminder: Our call is in 10 minutes. Zoom link is in your email. See you then. ``` **Note:** If you can't get their phone number, skip this. Email alone still gets 80%+ show-up. ## 4. Handle Cancellations Automatically --- **This is where most founders lose deals.** Someone cancels, you forget to follow up, deal dies. **Fix:** Create a Calendly Workflow that auto-sends a reschedule link.
**How to set it up:** 1. Go to Workflows → Create Workflow 2. Trigger: "Event is cancelled" 3. Wait: 5 minutes after cancellation 4. Action: Send email to invitee **Auto-Reschedule Email Template:Subject:** Want to pick a better time?
**Example:** ``` Hi {invitee_first_name}, saw that you are now unable to make our call. Here's our link to reschedule and find a more suitable time - {reschedule_url} Looking forward to speaking with you soon. [Your Name] ``` ![Screenshot 2024-03-17 at 11.24.44.png](../../../Sales/Qualifying%20Prospects%20&%20Sales%20Process/Sales%20Process/Screenshot_2024-03-17_at_11.24.44.png) ## 5. Pre-Call Guide ---
You will have **seen** we mentioned **including** a pre-call guide. You probably thought what’s that, how do I make one etc… That’s what we will cover in the next section

# Pre-Sell With A Pre-Call Guide # Pre-Sell With A Pre-Call Guide ---
**🚨QUICK NOTE:** Most founders book a call and then go silent until it happens. A simple pre-call guide can increase your close rate by 15-25% because prospects show up half-sold.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Understand what a pre-call guide actually is (and why it works) ✅ Know exactly what to include to build trust and set expectations ✅ Have a proven template you can customize and use immediately ✅ Know when and how to share it for maximum impact ## What a Pre-Call Guide Is ---
A pre-call guide is a simple document (usually 5-10 pages) that you send to prospects after they book a call with you.
**It's NOT a pitch deck.It IS a trust-building asset that:**
- Sets clear expectations for the call - Shows social proof and credibility - Gets them thinking about their situation - Eliminates common objections before you even talk - Makes them more committed to showing up
**Think of it as doing half your sales work before the call starts.** ## Why You Should Use One --- **What It Does:**
✅ Does most of the heavy lifting before the call starts ✅ Lets you reference sections during the call instead of explaining from scratch ✅ Gets them thinking about their situation beforehand ✅ Eliminates basic questions and objections upfront ✅ Makes prospects more committed (they've invested time learning about you)
Most of your prospects have never heard of you before booking the call. Without a pre-call guide, you spend the first 15 minutes of every call building credibility and explaining what you do. --- ## What to Include in Your Pre-Call Guide --- ### 1. What to Expect --- Brief overview of what the call will cover. Sets the agenda and reduces anxiety. ### 2. Why This Call is Worth Your Time --- 4-6 bullet points explaining the value they'll get. Focus on insights, not your services. ### 3. About Us/Meet The Team --- Quick credibility builder. How long you've been doing this, what you're proud of, team photo. ### 4. Who You'll Be Speaking With --- Personal introduction from you. Put a face to the name. Include LinkedIn connection CTA. ### 5. Frequently Asked Questions --- Address the 4-5 questions you get on every call. Price ranges, timeline, what's included, etc. ### 6. Success Stories (3-4 Case Studies) --- Real results for similar businesses. Include company type, specific outcomes, and what made it work. ### 7. ROI Breakdown --- Simple visual showing how working with you pays for itself. Keep it conservative. ### 8. Preparing For Our Call --- Tell them what to bring: current metrics, goals, budget range. Makes the call more productive. ## When and How to Share It --- **Timing:**
- Include the link in your immediate confirmation email - Mention it again in your 24-hour reminder
**Where to Host It:**
I recommend **Gamma** (gamma.app) - Super easy to use. This is what we built the template with. You could upload the template straght to Gamma and have it edit with AI (But use whatever works for you: Notion, Google Slides, Canva, even a simple PDF.)
## The Template --- **We've created a complete pre-call guide template you can copy and customise:** ![Screenshot 2025-08-03 at 10.29.14.png](Pre-Sell%20With%20A%20Pre-Call%20Guide/Screenshot_2025-08-03_at_10.29.14.png) ## Click Below To Download Your Template --- [Pre-Call-Guide-template.pdf](Pre-Sell%20With%20A%20Pre-Call%20Guide/Pre-Call-Guide-template.pdf) **What's Included:**
- All 8 sections with placeholder text - Professional design that works on any device - Ready to customise with your info **Setup Time:** 30 minutes to customise with your details. **Impact:** Does the credibility work before you even start talking.
## Final Note --- This isn't about being fancy. It's about doing the work upfront so your sales calls are easier and more profitable. ← Previous [Call-Show Up Process](/resources/call-show-up-process-24af6d94771f804d9389d8147a5a0020) Next → [Set The Frame + Own The Call](/resources/set-the-frame-own-the-call-24af6d94771f8068a9bee7932561182d) On this page

# Set The Frame + Own The Call # Set The Frame + Own The Call ---
**🚨QUICK NOTE:** Most people let prospects drive the call. Big mistake. If you don't set the agenda, they will. And their agenda is usually "tell me everything so I can think about it later." Your agenda should be "diagnose their situation and lead them to a decision."
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Set a clear agenda that keeps you in control of the call flow ✅ Build rapport without wasting time on small talk ✅ Lead the conversation through a proven structure ✅ Handle prospects who try to derail or rush the process ## Why Most Founders Lose Control --- **They skip the agenda setting.**
Without a clear structure, calls become chaotic. Prospects ask random questions, jump to pricing too early, or try to get you to "send something over" without qualifying themselves. **The result?** You spend 30 minutes answering questions about your service instead of diagnosing their problems. Then they say "sounds interesting, can you send me some information?"
It doesn’t have to be like that. --- ## How to Set The Agenda (The 30-Second Frame That Controls Everything) --- **Example:**
"Great. So [Name], as you’re looking for [your service] what I'd like to do is share some information about us and why we reached out. I'll ask a few questions to understand your business and what you're looking for. IF WE CAN HELP AND YOU’RE A FIT. I'll walk you through exactly what we do and I'll share details on the offer and we'll discuss next steps. Sound good?"
--- **Why this works:** - You've told them exactly what's happening - They feel in control (because you asked "sound good?") - But YOU are actually in control of the flow - You can refer back to this if they try to jump ahead ## How To Handle Common Disruptions --- ### "What do you guys do exactly?" "Great question - that's actually what I want to walk you through. Let me just ask a couple questions first to understand your situation, then I can show you exactly how we'd help someone in your position." ### "Can you just send me some information?" "Of course I can send something over. Just so I send the right information, help me understand [your situation/goals]. That way I can customise what I send." ### "What does this cost?" "Pricing depends on your specific situation. Let me understand what you're looking for first, then I can give you exact numbers that make sense for your business." ### "I need to think about it" "Of course - this is an important decision. What specifically do you need to think through? Maybe I can help clarify those points now." ## Common Mistakes That Kill Your Frame --- ❌ **Answering every question immediately** - They ask about pricing, you give pricing without context ❌ **Letting them control the flow** - "Actually, before we get into that, can you tell me about your guarantee?" ❌ **Not setting expectations** - You just start asking questions without explaining why ❌ **Rushing through discovery** - You want to get to your pitch instead of finding their pain ## Your Frame Control Checklist --- **Before every call, remind yourself:** - [ ] I will set the agenda in the first couple of minutes - [ ] I will not answer pricing questions until I understand their situation - [ ] I will not move to the solution until I find something to anchor against - [ ] I will get clear next steps before ending the call - [ ] I control this conversation while making them feel heard ## The Mindset Shift ---
**Old thinking:** "I need to impress them with how much I know" **New thinking:** "I need to diagnose their situation"
**Old thinking:** "I should answer all their questions" **New thinking:** "I should ask better questions than they've ever been asked"
**Old thinking:** "I hope they like my service" **New thinking:** "I need to understand if we're a good fit"
![you.png](Set%20The%20Frame%20+%20Own%20The%20Call/you.png) ← Previous [Pre-Sell With A Pre-Call Guide](/resources/pre-sell-with-a-pre-call-guide-24af6d94771f80e0b22bda6c56404c19) Next → [Call Prep - Set Yourself Up to Win](/resources/call-prep-set-yourself-up-to-win-24af6d94771f8094b936fecf5319b6ab) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/sales/.content:
# One-Call vs Two-Call Close # One-Call vs Two-Call Close ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Most founders think "one-call close" means you MUST close on that call. Wrong. It just means you present the offer AND attempt to close on that call. Big difference
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Understand the real difference between one-call and two-call approaches ✅ Know which method fits your offer ✅ Stop putting unnecessary pressure on yourself to "close or die" ✅ Choose the right framework for your experience level ## The Real Difference --- ### One-Call Close ---
**What it actually means:** You present your offer and attempt to close on the same call. **What it DOESN'T mean:** They have to say yes or the call failed. **Key insight:** You're giving them everything they need to make a decision. Whether they decide on the call or need time is secondary.
### Two-Call Close ---
**What it means:** You split discovery and solution presentation across two separate calls. **When this works:** Complex offers that need research, custom proposals, or when you need time to prepare a tailored solution.
## Which One Should You Use? --- ### Choose One-Call Close If: ---
- **You're selling standard lead gen** (pay-per-appointment, monthly retainer) - **Your offer is straightforward** - prospects can understand it quickly - **You're targeting qualified leads** who know they need what you sell - **You want efficiency** - more calls, faster decisions **Example:** "We generate 20 qualified appointments per month for $3K. Here's how it works, here are the results, here's the price."
### Choose Two-Call Close If: ---
- **You're selling complex systems** (full sales funnels, multi-service packages) - **You need to do research** between calls to customize your approach - **Your prospects need education** about what you do - **You're newer to sales** and want less pressure per call **Example:** Custom growth consultancy that requires analysing their business model before proposing a solution.
## One-Call Close Wins For Most People --- **Lets look at Lead Gen Service as the example. You get on a call and a prospects expect to understand:**
- What you'll deliver - How you'll deliver it - What it costs All of this can be explained in one call. For you to force the prospect to sit through a 30min initial call and then tell them you will book another call to share the details including pricing etc for a straigthforward effort is ridiculous. You need to realise you’re doing it because it makes you feel better, more comfortbale, less pressured. Its not better for the prospect.
## Common Mistakes --- ❌ **Choosing two-calls because you're scared** - If you can explain your offer in one call, do it ❌ **Forcing one-call when you need research** - Don't guess at solutions; get the info you need first ❌ **Thinking one-call means high pressure** - It's about efficiency, not aggression ❌ **Using two-call to avoid objections** - You'll get the same objections on call two ## The Bottom Line --- **For most agency offers:** One-call close is the way to go. **Why it works:** - Prospects are ready to understand your solution - You can present everything they need to know - You maintain momentum from their initial interest - You find out faster if they're serious or just browsing **Remember:** One-call close is about presenting the complete offer and attempting to close. Whether they decide immediately or need a follow-up call is less important than maintaining control of the process. ← Previous [The $30K/Month Call Framework](/resources/the-30k-month-call-framework-245f6d94771f8061ae83f77891848e09) Next → [The $30K/Month Call Framework](The%20$30K%20Month%20Call%20Framework%20(Walkthrough)%2024af6d94771f8073bd75fc0a487a904f.md) On this page

**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** This call framework has been developed over 16 years and is the exact one me, my team, our clients all use to close 60%+ of qualified calls.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Know the exact structure to follow on every sales call ✅ Understand what you're trying to achieve in each section ✅ Have clear goals for each part of the conversation ✅ Stop winging calls and start controlling them ## The $30K/Month Call Framework - Sections Overview --- 1. **Rapport & Agenda Setting** - Take control from minute one 2. **Introduction** - Build authority before they can doubt you 3. **Discovery & Diagnosis** - Find the anchor you'll use to close them 4. **Transition** - Connect their pain to your solution 5. **Present The Solution** - Show them exactly how you solve their problem 6. **ROI Breakdown** - Make the financial case undeniable 7. **Present Your Price** - Position yourself as the obvious choice 8. **Handle Objections** - Turn concerns into reasons to buy 9. **Set The Next Step** - Control what happens next --- ## Complete Framework - Section Breakdown --- ## 1. # Breakdown The ROI # Breakdown The ROI ---
**🚨 GOAL/PURPOSE OF THIS SECTION:** Make the ROI undeniable, not just in isolation, but compared to every other alternative they’re considering and haven’t even spoke about. **NOTE: We are using Lead Generation as a service for this example to demonstrate it but you should amend to your specific offer.**
## Why It's Important: --- Your prospect is **already** weighing options. If you don’t bring up the comparison points, they will do it themselves after the call, without you. That’s how you lose good-fit leads to internal hires, cheaper agencies, or “let’s wait a quarter.” This section gives you control of that mental debate **before** it happens. ## Structure: --- ## **Step 1: Use Their Numbers**
Reference their close rate, LTV, and volume needs (from discovery).
## **Step 2: Show the ROI Math** ---
Walk through the basic return they'd get from your system using their numbers.
--- ## **Step 3: Compare to Alternatives** --- Walk through realistic alternatives:
- Hiring a full-time rep or team - Running ads - Buying leads / pay-per-call vendors - Doing nothing
## Step 4: Show Why Your Path Wins ---
It doesn’t just have to mean price! It could be: Faster, less risk, no internal resources, more control, clearer outcomes etc.
## Example Script Framework: ---
*“Let’s run the numbers quickly, using your own metrics.”* - *You told me your client value is $X* - *Your close rate is Y%* - *And you want to hit [goal/timeline]* *“With our system, we’ll deliver [X result] in [Y time]. At your current conversion and value, that would mean [Z outcome].”* *“Now let’s compare that to your other options.”*
## Comparison Examples (Choose What Fits): ---
Again, this example uses Lead Generation as a service to better demonstrate but you should identify prospects alternate options based on your service
### **1. Hiring Internally** ---
- “A decent SDR/Closer/Operator/Strategist costs $4–8K/month all-in.” - You have ~$3K recruitment cost - “Hiring + onboarding = 4–6 weeks minimum.” - “And you’ll still need systems, SOPs, lead sources, coaching, etc.” - “It’s more cost, more risk, and slower to ROI.”
### **2. DIY / Internal Build** ---
- “You’d need to build [system/process/team] yourself but you told me that’s the exact bottleneck right now.” - “You’ve already tried doing this internally and it’s costing [time/revenue/missed opportunity].”
### **3. Buying Leads / Cheap Vendors** ---
- “You could outsource this to a cheaper agency but you’ve seen the quality issues and lack of control that comes with that.” - “It might feel cheaper up front, but it costs more long term in bad-fit clients and churn.”
### **4. Paid Ads** ---
- “You’ll likely spend $3–5K/month in testing just to figure out if it converts and even then, you still need to call leads and close them.” - “If your current close rate is a bottleneck, paid ads just pour water into a leaky funnel.”
### **5. Doing Nothing** ---
- “Staying where you are is already costing you [$X per month] in [lost revenue / churn / missed goals / personal stress].” - “That’s the most expensive path of all.”
---
💡 **Reminder:** Your buyer is already thinking this way. You’re just saying it out loud first — and guiding how they weigh the options.
## Framing Transition: ---
**“So this isn’t about whether you do something, it’s about how you solve it.”** - Hire slowly and hope they ramp? - Try to build it all internally? - Or use us and start getting results within [timeframe].

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/sales/.content:
# Discovery & Diagnosis # Discovery & Diagnosis ---
**🚨 GOAL/PURPOSE OF THIS SECTION:** Find the anchor. A specific pain point or goal you can leverage throughout the rest of the call, WHILE naturally qualifying them to ensure they're a fit for your service.
## Why It's Important: --- This is THE most critical section. Without finding something specific to anchor against, you'll have no leverage when presenting your solution or handling objections. But you also need to qualify them during this conversation, not through a boring interrogation. ## The Dual Objective: --- **1. Find the Anchor** - A specific, measurable pain/goal **2. Qualify Them** - Make sure they meet your ICP ## Qualify Based On What You Actually Need (Lead Gen Example): ---
Too often people will ask endless quesitons to qualify the prospect when in reality there’s probably 2-3 quesitons they MUST ask on the call because they can’t get that info anywhere else.
**Example of MUST ask questions:** - **Confirmed Target Market** (to assess TAM size) - **Client Lifetime Value** (to calculate ROI) - **Close Rate** (to show realistic expectations)
⚠️ Don't ask these as a list! Weave them naturally into the conversation while diagnosing their situation.
## Structure: --- ## **Step 1: Open-Ended Exploration**
*“So [Name], what's got you looking at [your service] right now?"* **If they say "just looking around":** *"Most people aren't getting on sales calls to look around, there's a reason right? It's either trying to achieve a goal or solve a problem. What's that for you?"*
## **Step 2: Dig Deeper While Naturally Qualifying** ---
**As you're uncovering their situation, sprinkle in qualifying questions conversationally:** - *"Okay so you're struggling to get enough leads... who exactly are you trying to target? Who are you looking to book calls with?"(This gets target market for TAM assessment)* - *"And when you do close a client, what's the typical value of that relationship over time?"(This gets CLTV for ROI calculations)* - *"What's been your experience with close rates? When you get on calls with prospects, how many typically convert?"(This gets close rate for realistic expectations)*
## **Step 3: Continue Digging for the Anchor** --- **Examples of good follow-up questions:**
- "What's driving that timeline?" - "What happens if you don't hit that goal?" - "How are you currently handling this?" - "What's the gap you're trying to fill?" - "How much revenue did that represent?"
### Real Conversational Example:
**Sean:** *"So what's got you looking at lead generation right now?"* **Prospect:** *"We've been struggling to get enough qualified calls"* **Sean:** *"Okay, and who exactly are you trying to target? Who are you looking to get on calls with?"* **Prospect:** *"B2B SaaS companies, usually above a million in revenue"* **Sean:** *"Got it. And when you were getting calls before, what was your close rate looking like?"* **Prospect:** *"About 20% typically"* **Sean:** *"And when you do close a client, what's the typical value of that relationship?"* **Prospect:** *"Usually around $20k over the lifetime"* **Sean:** *"So you need qualified calls with B2B SaaS companies. What's driving the urgency right now? Why are you looking to solve this?"* **Prospect:** *"Well, our biggest client just left..."(Now you're back to finding the anchor while having naturally qualified them)*
## Internal Qualification Checklist: --- **As the conversation flows, mentally or literally check off:**
✓ Target market has sufficient TAM (can you generate enough leads?) ✓ CLTV makes ROI compelling (is the investment worth it for them?) ✓ They have a close rate (will they actually convert the calls?) ✓ They have a specific anchor (goal/pain to leverage)
## **Quick ROI Formula (Use Their Numbers to Drive the Sale)** --- Once you’ve got their **CLTV** and **close rate**, use this to calculate ROI during the call: (or relevant numbers needed for your service)
> [# Projected Meetings] × [Close Rate %] × [LTV] = Projected Revenue > Then: > [Projected Revenue] ÷ [Your Price] = ROI Multiple >
### **Example:** ---
- 30 qualified calls - 20% close rate - $20K LTV → 30 × 0.2 = 6 clients → 6 × $20K = $120K revenue → $120K ÷ $4,500 = **26.7X ROI**
### ROI Calculator (optional):
You could even have a dev set up the Closing Clients ROI calculator we created which you can view here: [Sales & Marketing Assets](/resources/sales-marketing-assets-b8bc52a597034733862d71617324cbfd)
## The Transition Test: ---
Before moving forward, you should be confident they will say yes to: *"So if we could help you [achieve specific goal/solve specific problem] by [their timeline], that would solve what you're dealing with right now?"*
**They must say YES before you continue.** ## Common Mistakes to Avoid: --- ❌ Asking qualifying questions like an interrogation ❌ Moving forward without finding the anchor ❌ Accepting vague answers like "we want more leads" ❌ Not quantifying the problem with specific numbers ❌ Rushing through discovery to get to your pitch ❌ Asking too many questions - keep it conversational ## Success Indicators: --- ✅ You have specific, measurable pain/goal (the anchor) ✅ You understand their timeline and urgency ✅ You know the financial impact ✅ You've naturally qualified their target market, CLTV, and close rate ✅ The conversation felt natural, not like an interview ✅ They've confirmed solving this would be valuable ← Previous [Introduction](/resources/introduction-24af6d94771f80878fe7ddaf2185745e) Next → [The Transition](/resources/the-transition-24af6d94771f8046a1ddddd5378a1a29) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/sales/.title: Handle Objections
closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/sales/.content:
# Handle Objections # Handle Objections ---
**🚨 GOAL/PURPOSE OF THIS SECTION:** Address concerns while maintaining control and moving toward close.
## Why It's Important: --- Objections are buying signals, they're interested but need specific concerns addressed. ## Common Objections & Scripts: --- ### **Objection #1: *"I need to think about it"* Your response:**
*"I totally understand. When you say think about it, what specifically would you be thinking about? Is it the investment, the timing, or something about the process?" [Address specific concern] "Other than [specific concern], is there anything else that would prevent you from moving forward?"*
### **Objection #2: *"It's too expensive"*** ### **Your response:**
*"I understand investment is important. When you say expensive, expensive compared to what? You’re currently spending X to solve this problem/This problem is costing you $y." [Show cost of inaction] "The way I see it, you're either going to invest [$X] to solve this, or continue losing [$X] every month from [their problem]."*
### **Objection #3: "I need to discuss with my partner/team"** ### **Your response:**
*That makes complete sense. What do you think [partner] would need to see to feel comfortable moving forward?"* *"What concerns do you think they might have?"* *“Would it help if I sent over a quick summary for [partner] with the key outcomes and ROI we walked through?”*
## The Objection Framework: --- 1. **Acknowledge:** "I understand..." 2. **Clarify:** "When you say X, what specifically do you mean?" 3. **Address:** Handle the specific concern 4. **Confirm:** "Does that address your concern?" 5. **Close:** "Other than that, is there anything else preventing us from moving forward?" ## Common Mistakes to Avoid: --- ❌ Getting defensive about objections ❌ Not finding the real objection behind the surface objection ❌ Talking too much instead of asking questions ❌ Not confirming you've addressed their concern ## Success Indicators: ✅ Prospect provides specific details about their concerns ✅ They're engaging in problem-solving mode ✅ Questions become more logistical than emotional ← Previous [Present Your Price](/resources/present-your-price-24af6d94771f8008be79eeb6e6eb77c2) Next → [Set The Next Step](/resources/set-the-next-step-24af6d94771f80a39d84c689d24d48bf) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/sales/.content:
# Introduction # Introduction ---
**🚨 GOAL/PURPOSE OF THIS SECTION:** Establish credibility and explain why you reached out while positioning yourself as the expert.
## Why It's Important: --- Before asking discovery questions, prospects need to understand who you are and why they should answer your questions. Without credibility, they'll be guarded. ## Structure: --- ## **Step 1: Company Credibility**
*I'm [Name], founder of [Company]. We've been in business for [X] years and currently [team size]. Over the past [timeframe], we've worked with [number] of [specific type] agencies and developed a system that specifically works for [their type] of business."*
## **Step 2: Proud Results Positioning**
*"We're really proud of the work we've done - we've helped companies like [specific example] achieve [specific result]. What our system allows us to do is [main benefit/guarantee]."*
## **Step 3: Address Qualification Concerns Early**
*"Now I'm sure one of your first questions is 'how do we make sure these leads are actually qualified?' because that's the most important thing, right?"*
## Example Script:
*"I'm [Name], founder of [Company]. We've been in business for 3 years, currently a team of 3, and we're really proud of the work we've done. Over the past few years we've worked with over 30 paid ad agencies and developed a system that specifically works for digital marketing agencies. What we've seen across all our clients is that we can absolutely guarantee 24 triple-qualified appointments in 90 days. For example, we helped JBI Advertising book 44 qualified meetings in just 60 days."*
## Common Mistakes to Avoid: --- ❌ Making it too long (over 2 minutes) ❌ Not mentioning specific numbers or results ❌ Talking about features instead of outcomes ❌ Not addressing the qualification question early ## Success Indicators: --- ✅ Prospect asks follow-up questions about results ✅ They seem engaged and interested ✅ Natural flow into explaining qualification process ← Previous [Rapport & Agenda Setting](/resources/rapport-agenda-setting-24af6d94771f8057bc1af65fda42573e) Next → [Discovery & Diagnosis](/resources/discovery-diagnosis-24af6d94771f80dba33ac44dbc937a23) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/sales/.title: Present The Solution
closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/sales/.content:
# Present The Solution # Present The Solution ---
**🚨 GOAL/PURPOSE OF THIS SECTION:** Show the mechanism of HOW you'll solve their specific problem and set clear expectations about deliverables and timeline.
## Why It's Important: --- Prospects need to understand exactly how you'll achieve the outcome they want and how much of their time will be required (which should be minimal). This builds confidence in your process. ## Structure: --- ## **Step 1: Tie Back to Their Situation**
*"So [Name], based on what you've shared about [specific problem/goal], here's exactly how we'd solve that for you...”*
## **Step 2: Explain the Mechanism (The HOW)** --- **Walk through your actual process step-by-step:**
- What you do on their behalf - Timeline for setup and delivery - How much of their time is required - What they can expect and when
--- ## **Step 3: Set Deliverable Timeline** ---
Be specific about what happens when and their minimal involvement required.
## Step 4: Transition to Offer Overview ---
*“So like I mentioned in our email, our offer is [X] with [Y] guarantee. Let me break this down for you...”*
## Example Script Framework (Lead Gen Example): ---
*"So [Name], based on what you've shared about [needing X qualified appointments to achieve Y goal], here's exactly how we'd solve that for you.* *First, we'll purchase [X number] of domains on your behalf and set up all the technical infrastructure. When you sign the agreement, everything will be ready within 48 hours - that includes all the copy, the targeting, everything.* *We'll then schedule a brief onboarding call with you to finalise the targeting criteria and messaging, this takes about 45 minutes of your time.* *From there, we target companies that match your exact ICP - [reference what they told you in discovery] - and we handle all the outreach, follow-up sequences, and appointment booking directly into your calendar.* *The only thing you need to do is show up to the qualified calls we book for you. That's it.* *So like I mentioned in our email, our offer is [X qualified appointments] with a [contractual guarantee]. Let me break this down for you..."*
## Key Elements to Include: --- 1. **Technical setup** - What you handle on their behalf 2. **Timeline** - How quickly you can get started 3. **Their minimal involvement** - Make this minimal 4. **Ongoing process** - What happens day-to-day 5. **Their only responsibility** - Show up to calls 6. **Transition to offer** - Bridge to the next section ## Common Mistakes to Avoid: --- ❌ Being vague about the actual process ❌ Not setting clear timeline expectations ❌ Making it sound like they need to do a lot of work ❌ Skipping the transition to your formal offer ❌ Talking about features without connecting to their outcome ## Success Indicators: --- ✅ Prospect understands exactly what you'll do ✅ They realize how little work is required from them ✅ Questions about logistics rather than "how does this work?" ✅ Natural flow into discussing your guarantee/offer ✅ They seem relieved about the minimal time commitment ← Previous [The Transition](/resources/the-transition-24af6d94771f8046a1ddddd5378a1a29) Next → [Breakdown The ROI](/resources/breakdown-the-roi-24af6d94771f8028a833d6dfbd0c32d6) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/sales/.title: Present Your Price
closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/sales/.content:
# Present Your Price # Present Your Price ---
**🚨 GOAL/PURPOSE OF THIS SECTION:** Present pricing confidently while using value stacking to make the investment feel like a bargain compared to everything they're getting.
## Why It's Important: --- How you present price affects perception of value. Value stacking turns your service from "expensive" into "I'd be crazy not to take this.” ## Structure: --- ## **Step 1: Reference the ROI**
Tie back to the financial return you just calculated
## **Step 2: Value Stack Your Offer** ---
Break down everything they're getting with market values (Like we went through here [Positioning: Sell More & Charge More](/resources/positioning-sell-more-charge-more-219f6d94771f80c48fa7d66bd87f028d))
--- ## **Step 3: Present Your Price as a Discount** ---
Show your price vs. total market value
## Step 4: Tie Back to Their Situation ---
Connect this investment to solving their specific problem
## Example Script Framework: ---
*“So based on the ROI we just calculated [$X] in new revenue from solving [their specific problem] let me break down exactly what you get when you work with us.”* *“If you were to piece this together yourself, here’s what you’d need to cover:”* - ***Email Deliverability Optimisation** – Monitoring + ongoing optimisation of your sending domains, plus internal guides for maintenance — $500/month* - ***Monthly Lead Generation** – Custom campaigns with targeting logic, list-building, and setup — $3,000/month* - ***Lead Nurturing** – Warming sequences and logic for long-cycle buyers — $1,500/month* - ***Inbox Management** – We handle all replies, chase no-shows, and book qualified meetings — $1,500/month* - ***Training Guides and SOPs** – For your internal team to scale what we install — $1,000/month* - ***Marketing Content Creation** – Cold email messaging, subject lines, angles — $500/month* - ***Unlimited Verified & Qualified Lead Lists** – Hand-built, always-fresh data sources for your exact ICP — $800/month* ***That’s $8,800/month in total market value.*** ***But your investment with us is just: $4,500/month.** That’s nearly 50% less than if you built this internally or went to market and we do it faster, with less risk, and guarantee outcomes.*
### Value Stacking Template: ---
Again we’re using Lead Gen as the example service. Amend this to reflect your own service
| **Service Component** | **Description** | **Market Value** | | --- | --- | --- | | Email Deliverability Optimisation | Monitoring and optimisation of your domains to ensure deliverability + step-by-step guides for internal upkeep | $500/month | | Monthly Lead Generation | Targeted, list-built lead gen campaigns with campaign logic | $3,000/month | | Lead Nurturing | Sequences and logic to warm up long-cycle buyers over time | $1,500/month | | Inbox Management | We manage replies, chase no-shows, and book qualified meetings | $1,500/month | | Training Guides and SOPs | Assets for internal teams to scale and replicate results | $1,000/month | | Marketing Content Creation | Foundational cold email messaging, subject lines, angles, etc. | $500/month | | Unlimited Verified & Qualified Lead Lists | Always-fresh data sources curated for your ICP | $800/month | ## Common Mistakes to Avoid: --- ❌ Presenting price without showing the value breakdown ❌ Making up unrealistic market values ❌ Not tying the investment back to their specific situation ❌ Rushing through the value stack ## Success Indicators: --- ✅ Prospect says "that's actually reasonable" or similar ✅ They're taking notes on what's included ✅ Questions shift from "how much?" to "when can we start?" ✅ No immediate price shock reaction ✅ They're asking about terms or logistics rather than questioning cost ← Previous [Breakdown The ROI](/resources/breakdown-the-roi-24af6d94771f8028a833d6dfbd0c32d6) Next → [Handle Objections](/resources/handle-objections-24af6d94771f80839667d313befed9a4) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/sales/.content:
# Rapport & Agenda Setting # Rapport & Agenda Setting ---
**🚨 GOAL/PURPOSE OF THIS SECTION:** Build immediate connection and take control of the call direction from the start.
## Why It's Important: --- Most prospects will try to control the conversation. Without setting the agenda, you'll get pulled into answering questions before understanding their situation, losing leverage and control. ## Structure: --- ## **Step 1: Quick Rapport Building (~30-60 seconds)**
- Location-based connection: "Where are you based?" - Light conversation about travel, weather, or business location - Keep it brief but genuine
## **Step 2: Set the Agenda (The Frame)**
*"Thanks for jumping on [Name]. Today what I'd like to do is share some information about us and why we reached out. I'll ask a few questions to understand your business and what you're looking for. I'll walk you through how we typically help businesses like yours. If it makes sense, I'll share details on how we could work together, and if it's a fit, we can discuss next steps. Sound good?"*
## Common Mistakes to Avoid: --- ❌ Spending too long on rapport (over 2 minutes) ❌ Not setting agenda and letting prospect control the call ❌ Being too rigid with the script vs. conversational ## Success Indicators: --- ✅ Prospect agrees to the agenda ✅ You maintain control of call flow ✅ Natural transition into introduction ← Previous [The $30K/Month Call Framework (Walkthrough)](../The%20$30K%20Month%20Call%20Framework%20(Walkthrough)%2024af6d94771f8073bd75fc0a487a904f.md) Next → [Introduction](/resources/introduction-24af6d94771f80878fe7ddaf2185745e) On this page

# Set The Next Step # Set The Next Step ---
**🚨 GOAL/PURPOSE OF THIS SECTION:** Get a clear commitment for next action, never leave a call without defined next steps.
## Why It's Important: --- Calls without clear next steps lead to ghosting and lost deals. You must control the follow-up process. ## Structure: --- ### **For Decision Makers Ready to Move Forward:**
*"Great, so it sounds like this makes sense for you. The next step would be getting the agreement sent over. I can have that to you within the hour. How quickly could you review and sign that?"*
### **For Non-Decision Makers:**
*"So based on everything we've discussed, this sounds like it would solve [their problem]. You mentioned you need to discuss with [decision maker]. How long do you think that conversation would take?" "Let's schedule a time to connect once you've had that discussion. How does [specific day/time] work?"*
### **For Those Who Need Time:**
*"I understand you want to think it over. Just to be clear, when you say think about it, you're thinking about [specific concern we discussed], right?"* *"How long do you think you'd need to work through that?"* *"Let's schedule a follow-up call for [specific time]. How does [day/time] work for you?"*
## The Calendar Close: --- **Always try to book the next call on the spot:**
*"Let me just pull up my calendar... I have [day] at [time] or [day] at [time]. Which works better for you?"*
## Follow-Up Email Template: ---
*"Thanks for the great conversation today. As discussed, I'm sending over [what you promised].* *Based on our conversation about [their specific situation], I believe this could really help you achieve [their goal].* *Looking forward to our call on [scheduled time] to discuss next steps."*
## Common Mistakes to Avoid: --- ❌ Ending calls without clear next steps ❌ Not getting specific dates/times for follow-up ❌ Leaving it vague like "I'll follow up next week" ❌ Not sending promised materials immediately ## Success Indicators: --- ✅ Specific date and time scheduled for next interaction ✅ Clear understanding of what happens next ✅ Prospect commits to specific action ← Previous [Handle Objections](/resources/handle-objections-24af6d94771f80839667d313befed9a4) Next → [The $30K/Month Call Framework (Walkthrough)](../The%20$30K%20Month%20Call%20Framework%20(Walkthrough)%2024af6d94771f8073bd75fc0a487a904f.md) On this page

# The Transition # The Transition ---
**🚨 GOAL/PURPOSE OF THIS SECTION:** Bridge from discovery to solution presentation using their specific situation.
## Why It's Important: --- This is where you use the anchor you found to create urgency and relevance for your solution ## Structure: --- ## **The Transition Formula:**
*"So [Name], based on what you've told me about [specific situation], and the fact that you need [specific outcome] by [timeline], let me show you exactly how we could help you achieve that."*
## Example Scripts Based on Discovery: --- ### **Example 1 - Lost Client:**
*"So Andrew, based on what you've told me about losing your biggest client and needing to replace that 40% revenue gap with about 10 new clients by end of Q2, let me show you exactly how we could help you achieve that."*
### **Example 2 - Growth Goal:**
*"So based on what you've shared about wanting to add 2 new clients per month to hit your growth targets, and you've got a 20% close rate, you'd need about 10 qualified appointments per month. Let me show you how we guarantee exactly that."*
## Common Mistakes to Avoid: --- ❌ Generic transitions not tied to their specific situation ❌ Not referencing the anchor you found ❌ Making it about your service instead of their outcome ## Success Indicators: --- ✅ Prospect is nodding along ✅ They're engaged and ready to hear your solution ✅ Clear connection between their problem and your solution ← Previous [Discovery & Diagnosis](/resources/discovery-diagnosis-24af6d94771f80dba33ac44dbc937a23) Next → [Present The Solution](/resources/present-the-solution-24af6d94771f805a9859fd1972aae48b) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/scale-.content:
# Know Your Capacity: How Many Clients Can You Handle? # Know Your Capacity: How Many Clients Can You Handle? ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Not knowing your capacity is the reason you pause outreach, clients churn and you struggle to scale.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Understand what capacity is ✅ Know your current capacity ✅ See exactly what breaks ✅ Understand how to increase capacity --- ## What Is “Capacity”? --- **Simply:**
It’s the number of clients you can take on right now, with your current systems, processes, and team.
**If you go beyond it:**
You pause outreach. You struggle to deliver. Clients start churning.
All you need for everything to fall apart is to have just one more client than your system can handle. --- ## You Don’t Always Need To Hire To Increase Capacity ---
Most founders think increasing capacity = hire someone. But often you don’t need to touch headcount. You just need to **reduce operational drag.**
### Example:
Right now, your onboarding might looks like this: - You send agreemt - Chase agreement - Send invoice - Chase invoice - Send onboarding form - Chase info for onboarding That’s ~**8 hours of admin** per client, **just to get started.**
**Now imagine this instead:**
- Clients hits a landing page - Watches a 2-min onboarding video - Signs the agreement on next page - Pays on page after that - Fills out a 4 question onboarding form - Auto-book their onboarding call That’s **8 hours saved** per client.
Do that across 5 clients, you’ve just **bought back a full work week** without hiring anyone.
**Capacity isn’t just about people. It’s about process.**
--- ## Step 1: Know Your Delivery Capacity --- How many clients can you serve without your quality dropping? **Use this calculation:**
- **Hours you work on delivery per week:** `___` - **Hours each client needs per week:** `___` - **Max clients = Hours per week ÷ Hours per client**
### Example: ---
- You have **20 hours per week** available for delivery - Each client needs **2.5 hours per week** **→ Max clients = 20 ÷ 2.5 = 8 clients**
**➡️ Figure out your max no. of clients now:** `_____` max clients --- ## Step 2: Know Your Sales Capacity --- How many new clients can you actually close per month?
**Use your real numbers:** - Leads you contact/month: `___` - Lead-to-call ratio: `___` (e.g. 1 booked call per 200 leads) - Show rate: `___%` - Close rate: `___%` **Monthly new clients =** `(Leads ÷ Lead-to-call ratio) × Show rate × Close rate`
### Example: ---
- 5,000 leads/month - 200 leads per booked call → 25 calls - 80% show rate → 20 showed calls - 25% close rate → 5 clients/month
➡️ **Write your number:** `_____` new clients/month --- ## Step 3: Spot Your Bottleneck/s --- Now compare delivery and sales capacity.
**Your Inputs:** - Max delivery clients: `___` - Current active clients: `___` - New clients/month: `___` **Time to hit delivery cap =** `(Max clients - Current clients) ÷ New clients/month`
### Example: ---
- Max delivery = 15 clients - Current = 8 - New/month = 3 → (15 - 8) ÷ 3 = **2.3 months** **Bottleneck = Delivery**
--- ## Step 4: Take the Right Action --- Only fix what’s breaking. Don’t over-engineer. ### If Your Bottleneck Is Delivery: --- You’re getting clients faster than you can handle them.
✅ Raise prices ✅ Hire delivery help (VA, contractor, part-time) ✅ Productize your service ✅ Fix inefficiencies like onboarding
**Pick one. Not all.** --- ### If Your Bottleneck Is Sales: --- You can handle more clients, but you're not closing them.
✅ Fix your weakest conversion metric ✅ Increase outbound volume (more inboxes) ✅ Test sharper messaging or offers
--- ## Early Warning System --- Track these weekly to avoid collapse: | **Signal** | **Meaning** | **Action** | | --- | --- | --- | | 80%+ delivery capacity | You’re near the ceiling | Plan next hire or raise price | | Call volume spikes 30%+ | Sales is outpacing delivery | Check delivery workload | | 10+ showed calls/month | Outreach working | Make sure delivery can handle it | | Quality drops or churn rises | You’re already over capacity | Pause outreach immediately | ## Capacity Protection Rules --- **Rule 1:** Never go above 90% delivery cap **Rule 2:** Plan your next hire at 80% cap **Rule 3:** Fix only one bottleneck at a time **Rule 4:** Recalculate monthly --- ## Final Snapshot --- Write down these 4 numbers:
1. **Max clients I can handle:** `_____` 2. **Clients I close per month:** `_____` 3. **My current bottleneck is:** `_____` 4. **My next move is:** `_____`

# Run the System You've Built # **Run the System You've Built** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** You've built everything you need to hit $30K/month. Now just keep yourself accountable and don't let the system break.
--- ## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Know what to monitor each week to stay on track ✅ Spot when things are breaking before they hurt revenue ✅ Keep momentum without getting distracted by busy work ✅ Know when to ask for help (and what context to provide) --- ## What To Monitor Weekly ---
These are the only numbers that matter for hitting $30K:
### **Revenue Generation** **Are leads coming in consistently?**
- Check your campaign tracker - If leads drop 30%+ week over week → fix campaigns immediately
**Are you closing deals?**
- Update your pipeline tracker - If close rate drops below your baseline → review recent calls, fix your process
## **Operations Health** --- **Can you handle current workload?**
- Are clients getting delivered on time? - Are you stressed/working 60+ hours? - If yes to either → you're at capacity, time to hire or raise prices
## **Revenue Growth** --- **Are you maximizing current clients?**
- When did you last ask a client for an upsell? - When did you last ask for referrals? - If it's been 2+ months → you're leaving money on the table
--- ## When Things Break --- **Leads drying up:**
- Go back to your campaign performance tracker - Check what changed in the last 2 weeks - Scale what's working, pause what's not - If you can't figure it out → post in our 1:1 channel with your actual numbers
**Sales stalling:**
- Review your last 5 sales calls - What objections keep coming up? - Go back to your sales process and tighten it up - If close rate is still dropping → post numbers + process in our 1:1 channel
**Operations breaking:**
- Go back through your SOPs and see what's not being followed - Fix the processes - (don’t pause outreach - just make sure to fix the bottlenecks - If you can't handle current workload → hire now, don't wait
**Revenue stuck:**
- Check: when did you last upsell a client? - Check: are you asking for referrals systematically? - Go back to your revenue ladder and actually use it - If clients aren't buying upsells → survey them about what they need
--- ## Getting Help From Us --- **Don't post vague questions. Give real context:**
**Instead of:** "My campaigns aren't working" **Post this:** "Lead volume dropped 40% in 2 weeks. Campaign tracker shows: - Email campaign A: 2% reply rate (was 4%) - Same messaging, same targeting (if accurate and NOT a SINGLE variable wash changed) What should I check?"
**Instead of:** "I can't close deals" **Post this:** "Close rate dropped from 25% to 10% over 3 weeks. Last 5 calls: - 3 said 'too expensive' - 2 said 'need to think about it' - This is my process [share]. Only changed X in the past 3 weeks. How can we fix it?”
**The more specific your context, the better help we can give you.** --- ## Keep It Simple --- **This isn't about perfect tracking or detailed reports.** **It's about:**
- Noticing when shit breaks - Fixing it fast - Getting back to growing revenue
**If you're spending more than 20 minutes a week on "reviews" you're doing busy work, not business building.**
**Your time is better spent:** - Fixing what's broken - Scaling what's working - Growing revenue per client
--- ## You Have Everything You Need --- **Tools you've now got:**
- Campaign performance tracker - Pipeline tracker - Sales process - Operations mapped - SOPs for delivery - Revenue ladder system
**Knowledge you have:**
- Outbound math to sign clients - When to hire (revenue triggers) - How to scale - How to maximise client value
**Stop learning. Start executing.**
If something's not working, you know where to find the answer in this roadmap or you can get help from us directly.
## **Now let’s go hit $30K/month!** --- ← Previous [Build Your Revenue Ladder](/resources/build-your-revenue-ladder-24bf6d94771f800e86fed2264f6bcfed) Next → [Scale to $30K/Month](/resources/scale-to-30k-month-24af6d94771f809e9364d94bd8ccbd7e) On this page

# Understanding ‘Scaling’ # Understanding ‘Scaling’ ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Most agencies confuse growth with scaling. Growth is adding revenue. Scaling is adding revenue while your effort stays the same (or decreases).
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Rewire how you define scale ✅ Anchor into system logic (not sales logic) ✅ Choose a scale path that matches your working style ✅ Avoid the 3 biggest mistakes founders make --- ## Quick Reframe ---
### Scaling = System Output **Capacity × Conversion × Continuity = Scaling** If one of those is missing, you’re not scaling.
--- ## What Scaling Isn’t --- ❌ Selling more without knowing your delivery cap ❌ Hiring people to patch broken ops ❌ Adding complexity without output ❌ Automating chaos ❌ Reactively chasing revenue with no system --- ## What Scaling Is --- ✅ Your Client Acquisition Engine brings in qualified leads weekly ✅ You convert consistently without sales panic ✅ You deliver without stress, scope creep, or missed deadlines ✅ You track performance and iterate consistently ✅ Your business gets more stable as it grows
Scaling = doing the same thing more systematically. Not doing more things.
--- ## The $30K Scaling Loop Scaling is just **running this loop**:
**[ Engine → Replies → Sales → Clients → Delivery → Weekly Review → Adjust → Repeat ]**
--- ## The 3 Different Ways to Scale | Type | Good for... | How it works | | --- | --- | --- | | **Horizontal** | Founders more operations/back-end orientated typically (think Nick Abraham with 100+ clients) | Add more clients, build backend, delegate | | **Vertical** | Sales-first founders (more like Sean Longden who are looking to maximise the LTV of their offer and clients) | Charge more, add ascension offers, upsell | | **Hybrid** | Most solo operators | Balanced LTV + volume + small team + systems | You don’t need to commit to one forever. But you should be aware of the types. --- ## How Your Role Changes as You Scale
Scaling isn’t just business growth. It’s personal growth based on how you operate.
| Phase | Your Role | | --- | --- | | **1** | You manually run the system | | **2** | You document + optimise the system | | **3** | The system runs: you oversee + refine |
If you’re at Phase 3 revenue, still doing Phase 1 tasks, you’re the bottleneck.
--- ## Growth vs Scaling (Example)
> Two founders both hit $25K/month. >
| Founder A (Growth) | Founder B (Scaling) | | --- | --- | | Takes on 5 new clients in 30 days | Adds 3 clients to existing system | | Fulfillment breaks | Fulfillment already mapped | | Works 70hr weeks | Still working 5hr days | | Revenue spikes, but churns 2 clients next month | Revenue compounds with less effort | | Asks: “How do I fix fulfilment?” | Asks: “Where can the system run smoother?” |
Growth = brute force (fine if you’re trying to hit $10K/month) Scaling = controlled system output. (What gets you to $30K+ a Month)
## Next Step --- You now understand:
- Growth is effort-based. - Scaling is output-based. - And your system is the real asset — not your effort.
So before you add more leads or close more clients…
→ **You need to know your capacity.**
That’s what we will cover next. --- ← Previous [Scale to $30K/Month](/resources/scale-to-30k-month-24af6d94771f809e9364d94bd8ccbd7e) Next → [Know Your Capacity](/resources/know-your-capacity-how-many-clients-can-you-handle-24bf6d94771f80f9aacfd3ec58227c60) On this page

# Who to Hire, When, and Why # Who to Hire, When, and Why ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** You can run a lean team up to $30K/month. You've improved your offer, fixed client acquisition, mapped your systems, and built SOPs. Now you need the right people.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Know exactly which roles you need to reach $30K/month ✅ Understand when to hire each person based on your revenue ✅ See why you only need 2-3 key hires (not a big team) ✅ Get the hiring sequence that actually works --- ## The Reality: You Need Very Few People --- **Most founders think scaling means hiring lots of people.**
**The truth:** You can hit $30K/month with just 2-3 key hires.
**Why so few people?**
- Your systems now work without you. - Your SOPs mean work gets done correctly. - You're not hiring to patch broken processes. **You're hiring to multiply your capacity, not fix your problems.**
--- ## The Only 3 Hires You Need ---
**Note:** These recommendations are based on a lead generation agency example, but the principles apply broadly.
### Hire #1: Virtual Assistant (VA) ---
**When to hire:** At $2K-5K/month **What they do:** Handle all administrative tasks that eat your time **Who needs this:** Every agency, regardless of offer type
**Tasks:** - Client onboarding - Data entry and list building - Spreadsheet management - Basic admin work **Cost:** $5-10/hour ($800-1,200/month full-time)
--- [How To Hire A VA](/resources/how-to-hire-a-va-24bf6d94771f80db882efcb7a86a80a0) --- ### Hire #2: Inbox Manager ---
**When to hire:** At ~$5K/Month **What they do:** Handle all prospect responses and client communication **Who needs this:** Essential for lead-gen agencies, valuable for all agencies
**Why valuable for any agency:**
- 5-minute response times vs. 2-hour response times dramatically increase conversions - Frees up founder time for high-value work - Consistent, professional communication improves brand perception - Low cost for high impact on client experience
**Tasks:**
- Respond to prospect inquiries - Book qualified calls - Manage your calendar - Follow up sequences **Cost:** $6-12/hour ($1,000-1,400/month full-time) or pay per meeting booked (~$50 per meeting)
--- [How To Hire An Inbox Manager](/resources/how-to-hire-an-inbox-manager-24bf6d94771f80459fe1f5874ceb2fad) --- ### Hire #3: SDR/Cold Caller (Optional) ---
**When to hire:** At $15K-20K/month **What they do:** Expand your lead generation through cold calling **Who needs this:** Most B2B agencies can benefit from additional lead generation
**Why valuable for lead-gen agencies:**
- Email only captures 30-40% of available leads - Cold calling reaches the other 60-70% - No email warm-up period required - Faster client results = higher retention
**Why valuable for other agencies:**
- Proactive outreach vs. waiting for inbound leads - Direct conversation often converts better than email - Faster lead qualification and shorter sales cycles **Cost:** $1,500-$2,000/month (Plus commissions which you can determine based on margin)
--- [How To Hire An SDR](/resources/how-to-hire-an-sdr-24bf6d94771f809bbc18f1d532fd42fe) --- ## Hiring Sequence That Works ---
**Step 1 ($2K-5K/month):** Hire VA first **Why first:** Frees up 10-15 hours/week of admin work **Result:** You can focus on sales and client delivery
**Step 2 (~$5K/month):** Add Inbox Manager **Why second:** Prospect responses are now handled **Result:** Higher response rates and more booked calls
**Step 3 ($15K-20K/month):** Consider SDR (optional) **Why optional:** Only if you want to double lead generation capacity **Result:** Access to 60-70% more prospects through calling
--- ## Why This Lean Approach Works --- **Traditional agency thinking:**
❌ Hire account managers ❌ Hire project managers ❌ Hire copywriters ❌ Hire designers ❌ End up with 8-10 people
**Lean agency reality:**
✅ Systems handle project management ✅ SOPs ensure quality delivery ✅ You focus on strategy and growth ✅ 2-3 people maximum
**The difference:** Your systems do the work that other agencies hire people to do. --- ## Cost Breakdown to $30K/Month --- **Total monthly team cost:**
**VA:** ~$1,000/month **Inbox Manager:** ~$1,200/month **SDR (optional):** ~$1,750/month **Total:** ~$3,950/month team cost
**At $30K/month revenue:**
- Team cost = ~13% of revenue - Rest goes to you and business growth **Compare this to agencies with 8+ people eating 40-60% of revenue.**
--- ## Red Flags: Don't Hire These Roles Yet --- **Avoid hiring until $30K+/month:**
❌ **Account managers** (your systems handle this) ❌ **Project managers** (your SOPs handle this) ❌ **Copywriters** (use our email writing tool) ❌ **Closers** (you should be closing at this stage) ❌ **Marketing managers** (focus on one channel that works)
**Why avoid these:** Each additional hire reduces your profit and adds management complexity. --- ## Action Plan --- **Right now:**
✅ Calculate your current monthly revenue ✅ Identify which hire comes next based on revenue stage ✅ Go to that specific hiring page ✅ Start the hiring process this week
**Don't hire ahead of revenue. Don't hire to fix broken systems.** **Hire to multiply capacity when systems already work.** --- ← Previous [Build Ops That Don’t Break When You Scale](/resources/build-ops-that-don-t-break-when-you-scale-24bf6d94771f8078bae8e087671d3564) Next → [Build Your Revenue Ladder](/resources/build-your-revenue-ladder-24bf6d94771f800e86fed2264f6bcfed) On this page
