closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/scale-.content:
# Build Ops That Don’t Break # Build Ops That Don’t Break ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** This is where you stop reacting and start building systems that run without you.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Get the 3-step method to build ops that scale with you ✅ See why templates fail and what actually works instead ✅ Walk away with one mapped system ready for documentation ✅ Stop being the bottleneck in your own growth --- ## You're Not Getting Templates. Here’s Why. --- **You're probably expecting processes/SOPs to copy and paste.** Here's why you’re not getting that
❌ Templates are built to be generic so anyone can use them ❌ They’re not specific to YOUR business and as a result not OPTIMAL ❌ You won't know how to fix them when they stop working ❌ You'll still be dependent on someone else's thinking
**We're giving you something better:** A repeatable method to build custom ops. **Learn the method once. Use it forever.** **Build systems that fit your business exactly.**
--- ## The 3 Steps to Building Ops That Don't Break --- ## Step 1: Map Your System --- **What it does:** Shows you exactly how work flows in your business right now. **Why first:** You can't fix what you can't see. Most ops break because founders skip this step. --- [Map Your System](/resources/map-your-system-24bf6d94771f80198c41c5e96cae9d5a) --- ## Step 2: Build the SOPs --- **What it does:** Turns your map into step-by-step processes anyone can follow. **Why second:** Maps show you what happens. SOPs make it happen without you. --- [Build the SOPs](/resources/build-the-sops-24bf6d94771f80ba91bbe20efc7374e7) --- ## Step 3: Stress-Test --- **What it does:** Proves your SOPs work before you delegate them. **Why third:** Untested SOPs break when you hand them off. Test first, delegate second. --- [Stress-Test](/resources/stress-test-24bf6d94771f803fbc3de822aed13cfa) --- ## Why This Method Works --- **Most founders:**
❌ Copy someone's SOP ❌ Realise it doesn't fit ❌ Try to modify it ❌ Break it completely ❌ Go back to manual work
**You:**
✅ Map your actual process ✅ Build SOP for your reality ✅ Test until bulletproof ✅ Scale without breaking
## Action Plan --- **This week:** ---
**Step 1:** Map your delivery operations (start with your biggest bottleneck) **Step 2:** Build SOPs from your map using Scribe or Loom **Step 3:** Test the SOPs with someone else before delegating
**Don't skip steps. Don't try to systematize everything at once.** **Pick one workflow. Map it. Document it. Test it.** --- ← Previous [The 6 Functions That Must Work Without You](/resources/the-6-functions-that-must-work-without-you-24bf6d94771f8048b4bcea0906cf9b33) Next → [Build Ops That Don’t Break When You Scale](/resources/build-ops-that-don-t-break-when-you-scale-24bf6d94771f8078bae8e087671d3564) On this page

# The 6 Functions That Must Work Without You # The 6 Functions That Must Work Without You ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Every agency has these 6 functions whether you plan them or not. The question is: do they depend on you, or do they run systematically?
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Identify the 6 core functions in your business ✅ Diagnose which functions are founder-dependent ✅ See what each function looks like when it's systematic ✅ Know exactly where your ops are breaking --- ## The 6 Functions Every Agency Has ---
**1. Client Onboarding** **2. Service Delivery** **3. Client Communication** **4. Project Management** **5. Financial Operations** **6. Capacity Control**
**Important:** These aren't departments or roles to hire. They're functions that happen in your business every single day. Right now, you're probably doing all 6 manually. --- ## Function 1: Client Onboarding --- **What it covers:** Getting clients from "yes" to first deliverable **Founder-dependent looks like:**
- You personally send agreements and chase signatures - You email clients asking for passwords/assets - You schedule kickoff calls manually - Every client onboarding feels different
**System-dependent looks like:**
- Client signs contract and pays automatically - Onboarding form collects all required info upfront - Kickoff call auto-schedules via calendar link - Same process every time, regardless of client
**Example:**
If your onboarding changes for every client, you have a problem. You want a standardised process across all clients. This often means reviewing your offer and sticking to your deal structure to make your ops work.
--- ## Function 2: Service Delivery --- **What it covers:** Actually doing the work you're paid for **Founder-dependent looks like:**
- You wing each project based on "feel" - Quality depends on your energy/mood that day - No two clients get exactly the same process - You're the only one who knows what "done" means
**System-dependent looks like:**
- Documented workflow for core deliverables - Quality checkpoints built into the process - Anyone can pick up and complete work to standard - Clear definition of what constitutes completion
**Example:**
If you can’t explain your exact delivery steps, you dont have your ops mapped out and are putting more pressure on yourself.
--- ## Function 3: Client Communication --- **What it covers:** How information flows between you and clients **Founder-dependent looks like:**
- Clients message you directly on random channels - You respond reactively throughout the day - No boundaries on when/how clients can reach you - Communication interrupts your deep work constantly
**System-dependent looks like:**
- Designated communication channels and times - Structured update schedule (weekly reports, etc.) - Clear response time expectations set upfront - Communication that doesn't require your immediate attention
**Example:**
If a random client message derails your day, you have zero boundaries. You need one channel, one cadence, and clear expectations
--- ## Function 4: Project Management --- **What it covers:** Tracking progress and coordinating work **Founder-dependent looks like:**
- You keep all project details in your head - Deadlines are rough estimates you made up - You coordinate everything manually - Team members ask you for direction constantly
**System-dependent looks like:**
- All projects visible in one central system - Clear timelines with built-in buffers - Automatic handoffs between project phases - Team knows next steps without asking you
**Example:**
If you're the only one who knows what’s going on, you are the system. Your team should be able to track progress, hit deadlines, and move work forward without you.
--- ## Function 5: Financial Operations --- **What it covers:** Money in, money out, money tracked **Founder-dependent looks like:**
- You manually create and send each invoice - You personally chase late payments - Cash flow is a monthly surprise - No clear picture of profit per client
**System-dependent looks like:**
- Automated invoicing tied to project milestones - Payment reminders sent automatically with escalation - Real-time cash flow visibility - Profit margins tracked per client/service
**Example:**
Most founders trying to get to $30K don’t even do their accounts. This has to change if you want to scale.
--- ## Function 6: Capacity Control --- **What it covers:** Managing how much work you can actually handle **Founder-dependent looks like:**
- You say yes to clients until you're completely overwhelmed - No clear view of your actual delivery bandwidth - Growth happens accidentally (or not at all) - You pause outreach when you feel "busy"
**System-dependent looks like:**
- Clear capacity limits based on actual delivery hours - Early warning system before hitting capacity ceiling - Controlled growth that doesn't break delivery quality - Consistent prospecting regardless of current workload
**Example:**
If you're still saying yes to clients without checking delivery capacity, you're setting yourself up to fail.
--- ## The System Dependency Test --- **Score yourself honestly (0-2 points each function):**
**2 points:** Works perfectly without me being involved **1 point:** Works, but needs my regular oversight **0 points:** Completely depends on me doing it manually
**Your total score:** ___/12 --- **What your score means:**
- **10-12:** You have real operational infrastructure - **6-9:** Mixed systems with some critical gaps - **3-5:** Mostly manual with occasional processes - **0-2:** Everything depends on you personally
---
**Your lowest-scoring function is the one you fix first**
## Why These Functions Break Your Scale --- **At $5K/month:** You can manually handle all 6 functions for 3-5 clients. **At $30K/month:** Manual handling of 6 functions for 20+ clients = certain business collapse. **The reality:** You can't systematise everything overnight. But you need to identify which functions are breaking now vs. which will break when you grow. --- ← Previous [The Founder-Led Ops Bottleneck](/resources/the-6-functions-that-must-work-without-you-24bf6d94771f8048b4bcea0906cf9b33) Next → [Build Ops That Don’t Break](/resources/build-ops-that-don-t-break-24bf6d94771f80d397f9f9d47865839f) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/scale-.title: The Founder-Led Ops Bottleneck
closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/scale-.content:
# The Founder-Led Ops Bottleneck # The Founder-Led Ops Bottleneck ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** If your business stops when you stop, you don't own a business. You have a job that you can't quit.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ See the real cost of founder-dependent ops ✅ Understand why manual ops = income ceiling ✅ Shift from "I do the work" to "I design how work gets done" ✅ Stop being irreplaceable in your own business --- ## The Brutal Reality ---
**At $5K/month:** You are the ops. **At $30K/month:** Ops exist without you. **The problem?** Most founders try to scale themselves, not their systems.
**Here's what happens when you're the ‘ops’:**
❌ Every client onboarding needs your personal attention ❌ You can't take a day off without things breaking ❌ Growth means more work, not more profit ❌ You pause outreach because you're "too busy" ❌ Clients churn because delivery depends on your time
--- ## Why Every Attempt To Scale Breaks ---
**Scenario:** You close 3 new clients this month.
**Manual ops founder:**
- Rewrites onboarding emails 3 times - Jumps between client calls with no system - Stays up late to hit deadlines - Stops prospecting because "no bandwidth" - Burns out by month-end
**System-dependent founder:**
- New clients flow through existing onboarding - Delivery runs on documented processes - Keeps prospecting while clients get served - Scales without breaking
**The difference?** One built infrastructure. One stayed the infrastructure. --- ## The Cost of Being Irreplaceable --- When you're irreplaceable in your own business:
**Your Income Ceiling = Your Working Hours** - 40 hours/week = $5K/month cap - 60 hours/week = $10K/month cap - 80 hours/week = $15K/month cap **Beyond that? You break.**
**Real costs:**
- Can't take travel without losing clients - Every sick day costs you - Growth requires working weekends - Hiring doesn't help because only you can "do it right" - Your business value = $0 (who would buy a job?)
--- ## The Mindset Shift That Changes Everything ---
**Old thinking:** "I need to get better at delivery." **New thinking:** "I need to stop being the delivery system."
**Old thinking:** "I'll systematize later when I'm bigger." **New thinking:** "Systems are how I get bigger."
**Old thinking:** "Only I can do this right." **New thinking:** "If only I can do it, it's broken."
--- ## What "Ops" Actually Means --- Ops isn't about tools or templates.
**Ops = Work that happens correctly without you being present.**
**Examples:** | **Manual Ops (You)** | **System Ops (Without You)** | | --- | --- | | You handle every step of onboarding for each client | Clients complete onboarding automatically after signing | | You remember to follow up on late invoices | Automated payment reminders with escalation sequence | | You coordinate between team members | Clear handoff processes with built-in checkpoints | | You decide project priorities daily | Priority matrix that anyone can follow | ## The Infrastructure vs. Dependency Test --- **Ask yourself:** **If I disappeared for 2 weeks (no calls, no emails), what would:**
- Still work perfectly? ← **Infrastructure** - Completely break? ← **Dependency**
Most founders businesses are completley dependant upon them Which fine, initially. **But that’s the reason why they struggle to scale.** --- ## Why This Matters for $30K/Month --- **At $30K/month, you need to serve 6x more clients than at $5K/month.** **Manual ops don't scale 6x. They break at 2x.**
**The math is simple:** - $5K/month = ~3-5 clients - $30K/month = ~15-25 clients
**You cannot personally manage 20 client relationships the way you managed 3.** You need infrastructure that works without you, or growth kills your business. --- ## Next Step --- You now understand the problem Next, we need to identify exactly which functions must work without you. ← Previous [Understanding ‘Operations’](/resources/understanding-operations-24bf6d94771f8010b350f7ebf097f666) Next → [The 6 Functions That Must Work Without You](/resources/the-6-functions-that-must-work-without-you-24bf6d94771f8048b4bcea0906cf9b33) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/scale-.content:
# Understanding ‘Operations’ # Understanding ‘Operations’ ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** You already mapped your sales process. **Operations** is everything that happens after a client says “yes.”
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Understand what operations actually is (and isn't) ✅ See why ops is your path from to $30K/month ✅ Stop confusing "busy work" with real infrastructure ✅ Recognise the difference between you doing work vs. work getting done --- ## Quick Breakdown: --- In this section you'll see the words **ops**, **processes**, and **systems**. Here's exactly what each one means so you don't get confused.
**Operations** → All the important work your business needs to run without you. **Example:** Clients get onboarded, campaigns run, invoices paid, even if you're offline.
**Process** → The exact step-by-step way one piece of work gets done. **Example:** How a new client goes from "yes" → signed contract → kickoff booked.
**System** → A process powered by tools and/or people so it runs automatically. **Example:** Payment triggers onboarding form, Slack alert fires when complete, VA checks for missing info.
--- ## What Operations Isn't --- ❌ Using project management tools ❌ Having SOPs that nobody follows ❌ Creating more documentation ❌ Buying more software subscriptions ❌ Hiring people to do your manual tasks ❌ "Staying organised" --- ## What Operations Is --- ✅ New clients onboard without you sending a single email ✅ Projects move forward while you're asleep ✅ Quality stays consistent regardless of your mood ✅ Team members know what to do without asking you ✅ Problems get solved before they reach you ✅ Revenue grows while your working hours stay flat
Operations = things working **because of the system**, not **because of you**.
--- ## The Simple Test --- **Ask yourself:** "If I disappeared for 2 weeks, what would still work perfectly?" **That's your operations.** **Everything else is just you working harder.** --- ## Why This Matters for Scaling --- **At $5K/month:**
- 3-5 clients - You can personally manage everything - Manual work is sustainable
**At $30K/month:**
- 15-25 clients - You cannot personally manage everything - Manual work kills the business
**The bridge from $5K to $30K isn't working 6x harder.** **It's building operations that work 6x better.** --- ## Operations vs. You Working (Example) --- **Two founders both land a new client:** | **You Working** | **Operations Working** | | --- | --- | | You send contract via email | Client signs contract from landing page | | You chase signature for 3 days | Contract auto-reminds until signed | | You manually send invoice | Invoice auto-generates when contract signed | | You remember to follow up on payment | Payment reminders send automatically | | You schedule kickoff call | Client books kickoff call via calendar link | | **Total founder time: 4 hours** | **Total founder time: 0 hours** |
**Same outcome. Zero founder time vs. 4 founder hours.** **That's operations.**
--- ## The Real ROI of Operations --- **Most founders think:** "I'll build operations when I have time." --- **Reality:** Operations gives you time. --- **Example:** Your current client onboarding takes 8 hours of founder time per client.
**At 3 clients/month = 24 hours** **At 6 clients/month = 48 hours** **At 12 clients/month = 96 hours**
**Operations that handle onboarding automatically = 0 hours regardless of volume.** --- ## Why Most Founders Get This Wrong --- **They think operations means:**
- More complexity - More things to manage - More tools to learn
**Operations actually means:**
- Less complexity - Fewer things requiring your attention - Work happening without tools breaking
**The mindset shift:**
Operations isn't about adding more systems. It's about removing yourself from systems that already exist.

# Build the SOPs # Build the SOPs ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** You've mapped your system. Now we turn that map into step-by-step processes that anyone can follow without you.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Understand how SOPs fit into your operational system ✅ Know why SOPs are essential for scaling ✅ Learn the exact method to create SOPs that actually work ✅ Have a complete list of SOPs your agency needs to build --- ## How SOPs Fit Into Your Operations --- **Here's where you are in the process:**
**Step 1:** Understand what operations means ✅ **Step 2:** Map your delivery system ✅ **Step 3:** Build SOPs from your map ← **You are here** **Step 4:** Stress-test your SOPs
**Your map shows you what happens. SOPs make it happen without you.**
**Think of it like:** Your map is the recipe. SOPs are the step-by-step cooking instructions that anyone can follow to get the same result.
**Important distinction:** We focused on mapping your operations (post-sale), but you need SOPs for your entire business, including sales.
**Sales SOPs:** Document your sales process (discovery calls, proposals, closing) **Operations SOPs:** Document your delivery process (onboarding, service delivery, billing) **Think of it like:** Sales SOPs get you clients. Operations SOPs keep them happy and paying.
--- ## Why SOPs Are Essential --- **Without SOPs:**
❌ Every task requires your personal involvement ❌ Quality depends on who's doing the work that day ❌ You can't delegate because no one knows "how you do it" ❌ You're stuck doing everything manually forever
**With SOPs:**
✅ Tasks can get done consistently without you ✅ Quality stays the same regardless of who does the work ✅ You can delegate entire processes confidently ✅ You can scale beyond your personal capacity
**Simple truth:** SOPs turn you from a freelancer into a business owner. --- ## How to Create SOPs That Actually Work ---
There are many ways you can create SOPs and ultimately it will come down to what you prefer to do.
### Option 1: Using [Scribe](https://scribehow.com/) ---
**Best for:** Computer-based tasks **Tool:** [**Scribe**](https://scribehow.com/) **Cost:** Free version available **Time:** As long as the task takes
**How it works:** --- [https://www.loom.com/share/312294c98c2e4ac189333a7b134bf4b6?sid=8c7a2951-836a-4927-aba0-fe31f0119a0a](https://www.loom.com/share/312294c98c2e4ac189333a7b134bf4b6?sid=8c7a2951-836a-4927-aba0-fe31f0119a0a) ### Option 2: Video Recording with Loom ---
**Best for:** Easier explanations of tasks **Tool:** [Loom](https://loom.com/) **Cost:** Free version available **Time:** As long as the task takes
**How it works:** ---
See the video above as a prime example. In this we have essentially created an SOP on how you can use Scribe to create SOPs.
## SOP Template Structure --- **Use this structure for every SOP:**
**SOP Name:** [Process name] **What:** The specific process you're documenting **When:** When this process should be used **Why:** Why this process matters **What You Need Before:** - Tool access required - Credentials needed - Files or information required **Step-by-Step Process:** 1. [First step with specific action] 2. [Second step with specific action] 3. [Continue until complete] **Quality Check:** - How to verify the work was done correctly - What the final result should look like **Resources:** - Links to tools - Template files - Additional help
--- ## Example List of SOPs Your Agency Needs --- ### Sales Process SOPs --- □ Lead scraping process □ Call structure/approach □ Proposal creation + sending □ Contract sending and follow-up □ Deal closing process ### Client Onboarding SOPs --- □ Welcome email sequence □ Onboarding form collection □ Kickoff call process □ Asset collection from client □ Project setup in management tool ### Service Delivery SOPs --- □ Campaign creation process □ Lead list building □ Email verification □ Campaign setup and scheduling □ Quality check before launch ### Client Communication SOPs --- □ Weekly reporting process □ Response handling templates □ Client meeting preparation □ Update email templates □ Issue escalation process ### Project Management SOPs --- □ Project creation and assignment □ Timeline management □ Team coordination □ Deliverable reviews □ Client approval process ### Financial SOPs --- □ Invoice creation and sending □ Payment follow-up sequence □ Expense tracking □ Financial reporting □ Unpaid invoice handling ### Team Management SOPs --- □ VA hiring process □ Team member onboarding □ Performance reviews □ Time tracking requirements □ Team meeting structure ### Tool and System SOPs --- □ CRM/Pipeline tracker data entry □ File organisation system □ Domain management □ Email deliverability monitoring □ Backup and security procedures --- ## Start With Your Critical SOPs ---
**Don't try to create everything at once. Start with the SOPs that would allow you to most quickly delegate work you’re doing that you can outsource for less than $10/hour.**
## Quality Standards for Your SOPs --- **A good SOP:**
✅ Someone with basic skills can follow it without asking questions ✅ Produces the same result every time ✅ Includes quality checks to catch errors ✅ Takes less time to follow than to create
**A bad SOP:**
❌ Requires you to explain it ❌ People interpret steps differently ❌ No way to verify if it was done correctly ❌ Takes longer to follow than doing it yourself
--- ## Where to Store Your SOPs --- **Choose one central location for all your SOPs:**
**Notion:** Best for organised, searchable SOPs with templates **Google Drive:** Simple folder structure, easy sharing **Dropbox:** Good for video SOPs, reliable syncing
**Structure your SOP library:**
- Sales SOPs - Onboarding SOPs - Delivery SOPs - Communication SOPs - Financial SOPs - Team Management SOPs
**Make it accessible:** Share the main folder with your entire team so anyone can find any SOP quickly. --- ## Action Plan --- **This week:**
✅ Choose your first SOPs to create ✅ Use Scribe or Loom to record yourself doing it once ✅ Clean up the recording into a clear SOP ✅ Test it by having someone else follow it ✅ Fix any gaps or unclear steps

# Map Your System # Map Your System ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** You already mapped your sales process. This page maps what happens after that.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Understand what "mapping your system" actually means ✅ See the benefits of getting everything out of your head ✅ Have a visual example of what a mapped system looks like ✅ Know exactly how to map your own delivery operations --- ## What Do We Mean "Map Your System"? --- **Map = draw every step after a client says “yes.”**
You put the steps on one page, in order, from **start** to **finish**. You add arrows to show what happens next. You mark where people wait or get stuck.
**Before:** the steps live in your head. **After:** anyone can follow the path and see the slow spots.
**Tiny example:** “Client pays → welcome email → form sent → form back → kickoff call booked.”
--- ## What's the Benefit of Doing This? --- **Right now, everything lives in your head. That’s a problem:**
❌ You can't visualise the process ❌ You don’t follow a SET process everytime ❌ You can't delegate because no one else knows the steps ❌ It’s not easy for you to spot the bottlenecks ❌ You can't improve what you can't see
**When you map everything out:**
✅ Bottlenecks become obvious ✅ You can delegate the entire processes ✅ You fix root causes ✅ You can systematically improve each step
**Simple example:** You keep chasing late payments. Mapping shows you there's no follow-up system after invoices are sent. You implement that, track if it improves and review again. --- ## Example: What It Looks Like with $30K/Month Roadmap --- **Here's what our $30K roadmap system map looks like:** [https://miro.com/app/live-embed/uXjVJVppPBo=/?embedMode=view_only_without_ui&moveToViewport=-1542%2C-457%2C7447%2C4037&embedId=57401545272](https://miro.com/app/live-embed/uXjVJVppPBo=/?embedMode=view_only_without_ui&moveToViewport=-1542%2C-457%2C7447%2C4037&embedId=57401545272) ### **What you can see in this map:** ---
- Every stage from $30K mentality to Scaling to $30K/Month is mapped out - Every sub-section of the resources is mapped out - You can see the flow and how each section is connected
**This is what you're building for your agency.** --- ## How You Can Do It (Including Tools) --- ### Step 1: Choose Your Tool ---
[**Miro](https://miro.com/):** Best for detailed maps, free version available [**Whimsical](https://whimsical.com/):** Simpler, good for basic flowcharts
**Pick one. Don't overthink it.** --- ### Step 2: Start with Your Current System --- **Write down what actually happens after a client signs:** **Example steps:**
1. Client signs contract 2. Send welcome email 3. Send onboarding form 4. Wait for form completion 5. Schedule kickoff call 6. Conduct kickoff 7. Create project in Google Drive + assets 8. Purchase inboxes 9. Etc.
### Step 3: Map It Visually --- **In your chosen tool:**
1. Create a box for each step 2. Connect boxes with arrows showing the flow 3. Mark steps that require you personally (use red boxes) 4. Mark steps where things often get stuck (use yellow boxes)
### Step 4: Spot the Problems --- **Look for these red flags:**
🚨 **Too many red boxes** (everything depends on you) 🚨 **Steps with no clear trigger** (happens "when you remember") 🚨 **Long gaps between steps** (things fall through cracks) 🚨 **Steps that loop back** (rework because of poor quality)
**Count your red flags:** `_____` --- ## Summary Recap --- **What you just learned:**
**Mapping =** Getting your delivery process out of your head and onto paper **Benefit =** You can see problems clearly and fix them systematically **Method =** List your steps, draw them out, spot the red flags
**Your next action:** Pick a tool and map your delivery process from contract signing to client renewal. **Time needed:** 1-2 hours for your first complete map. --- ← Previous [Build Ops That Don’t Break](/resources/build-ops-that-don-t-break-24bf6d94771f80d397f9f9d47865839f) Next → [Build the SOPs](/resources/build-the-sops-24bf6d94771f80ba91bbe20efc7374e7) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/scale-.content:
# Stress-Test # Stress-Test ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Your SOP isn't ready until someone else can follow it without asking questions.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Test if your SOP actually works ✅ Know if you can delegate it safely ✅ Fix any problems before they cost you time ✅ Stop being the bottleneck --- ## What Is "Stress-Testing"? ---
**Simply:** It's checking if your SOP works when someone else follows it.
**If it doesn't work:** They ask you questions. Work doesn't get done right. You end up doing it yourself.
**You don't need a complex test. You just need to see if it breaks.** --- ## Test 1: The Question Test --- Can someone follow your SOP without asking questions? **Here's how:**
- **Find someone:** Pick anyone who hasn't done this task - **Give them the SOP:** Nothing else. No verbal help. - **Start a timer:** See how long it takes - **Count questions:** Write down every question they ask
### Example:
**SOP:** Client onboarding process **Tester:** Your VA **Questions asked:** 3 **Time taken:** 45 minutes (should be 30) **Result:** Failed. Too many questions.
--- ## Test 2: The Volume Test --- Does your SOP work when you're busy? **Here's how:**
- **Double your work:** If you normally onboard 2 clients, try 4 - **Use the SOP:** Have someone follow it exactly - **Check the results:** Is everything still done on time and correctly?
### Example: ---
**Normal volume:** 2 client onboardings per week **Test volume:** 4 client onboardings per week **Result:** Fell behind on day 3, missed 2 deadlines **Result:** Failed. Doesn't scale.
--- ## Fix What Breaks --- **If Test 1 fails (too many questions):**
✅ Add screenshots to unclear steps ✅ Write exact words to use ✅ Give examples of good/bad work ✅ Add decision rules ("if this, then that")
**If Test 2 fails (doesn't scale):**
✅ Break big tasks into smaller pieces ✅ Create templates for repeated work ✅ Remove unnecessary steps ✅ Add time limits to each step
**Then test again until both pass.** --- ## Ready-to-Delegate Checklist --- Your SOP is ready when:
□ Someone followed it with **0 questions** □ They finished in the **expected time** □ The result was **correct quality** □ It worked at **2x volume** □ You wrote down **who owns it**
**Only delegate when all boxes are checked.** --- ← Previous [Build the SOPs](/resources/build-the-sops-24bf6d94771f80ba91bbe20efc7374e7) Next → [Build Ops That Don’t Break](/resources/build-ops-that-don-t-break-24bf6d94771f80d397f9f9d47865839f) On this page

Create Upsells To Ascend Clients --- [Client Upsells](/resources/client-upsells-24bf6d94771f8028ac79edf8162399f3) ## 3. **Get More Clients Through Referrals** --- [**Build Client Referral Program**](/resources/build-client-referral-program-24bf6d94771f8069816ce295ab6d5702) ← Previous [What is a ‘Revenue Ladder’?](/resources/what-is-a-revenue-ladder-24bf6d94771f80988ef3c9d03b6f593c) Next → [Track & Optimise Your Revenue Ladder](/resources/track-optimise-your-revenue-ladder-24bf6d94771f80c3ba8af45052cc3b63) On this page

# Track & Optimise Your Revenue Ladder # **Track & Optimise Your Revenue Ladder** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Most agencies never build a revenue ladder let alone track if they're working
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Know the 4 metrics that actually matter ✅ Set up simple tracking that takes 5 minutes per month ✅ Run monthly reviews to spot problems early ✅ Take the right actions when numbers drop --- ## The 4 Metrics That Matter --- ### 1. **Average Client Value Per Month** **What it is:**
How much each client pays you on average per month.
**Example:**
- Client A: $2K core + $1K upsell = $3K/month - Client B: $2K core only = $2K/month - Client C: $2K core + $800 upsell = $2.8K/month - **Average: $2.6K/month**
**What good looks like:**
- **Month 1:** $2K (just core service) - **Month 6:** $2.5K+ (some upsells happening) - **Month 12:** $3K+ (systematic upsells working)
--- ### 2. **Upsell Success Rate** **What it is:**
Percentage of clients who buy additional services.
**How to calculate:**
(Clients with upsells ÷ Total clients) × 100
**What good looks like:**
- **Month 6:** 20%+ of clients have upsells - **Month 12:** 30%+ of clients have upsells
--- ### 3. **Referral Rate** **What it is:**
Percentage of clients who refer new business.
**How to calculate:**
(Clients who made referrals ÷ Total clients) × 100
**What good looks like:**
- **Month 9:** 15%+ of clients make referrals - **Month 18:** 25%+ of clients make referrals
--- ### 4. **Revenue Mix** **What it is:**
Where your money comes from each month.
**Track these percentages:**
- Core services: ___% - Upsells: ___% - Referrals: ___%
**What good looks like:**
- **Month 6:** 85% core, 15% upsells, 0% referrals - **Month 12:** 70% core, 25% upsells, 5% referrals - **Month 18:** 60% core, 30% upsells, 10% referrals
--- ## Simple Monthly Tracking (5 Minutes) --- **Your monthly tracker:** **This month:**
- Total clients: ____ - Average client value/month: $____ - Clients with upsells: ____ (____%) - Clients who referred: ____ (____%) - Total revenue: $____ - Revenue from upsells: $____ (____%) - Revenue from referrals: $____ (____%) **Compare to last month:**
**Traffic light system:**
🟢 Improved or stayed good 🟡 Declined slightly 🔴 Declined significantly
--- ## Monthly Review --- ### **If average client value is stuck at $2K 🔴** **This month's actions:**
1. **Pick 3 clients getting good results** - schedule upsell conversations 2. **Review your upsell offers** - do they solve real problems? 3. **Check your timing** - are you asking after 3+ months of results?
--- ### **If upsell rate is under 20% after 6 months 🟡** **This month's actions:**
1. **Client interviews** - ask "what's your next biggest challenge?" 2. **Script practice** - role-play upsell conversations 3. **Offer simplification** - focus on just 1 upsell that makes obvious sense
--- ### **If no referrals after 9 months 🔴** **This month's actions:**
1. **Results audit** - are clients actually getting clear wins? 2. **Ask systematically** - add referral request to monthly check-ins 3. **Incentive test** - try $500 referral bonus
--- ### **If 90%+ revenue from core services after 12 months 🔴** **This month's actions:**
1. **Revenue ladder audit** - are your upsells actually valuable? 2. **Client feedback** - survey what additional help they need 3. **Competitor research** - what services are others offering?
--- ## Quarterly Deep Dive --- **Every 3 months, review:** ### **Best clients analysis:**
- Which clients have highest monthly value? - What upsells did they buy and when? - How can we replicate this with others?
### **Missed opportunities:**
- Which happy clients haven't bought upsells? - Which clients haven't made referrals? - What's our plan to approach them?
### **Service performance:**
- Which upsells sell best? - Which upsells never sell? (consider killing them) - What new upsells should we test?
--- ## Warning Signs Your Revenue Ladder Is Broken --- **Stop and fix if:**
- Average client value hasn't increased in 6 months - Less than 15% of clients have upsells after 12 months - Zero referrals from clients over 9 months old - Upsell rate declining for 3+ months
**When this happens:**
**Stop adding new services** and fix what's broken: 1. Survey clients about satisfaction and needs 2. Focus on core service delivery quality 3. Simplify to 1-2 obvious upsells 4. Get systematic about asking for referrals
--- ## Your Action Plan --- **Set up tracking:**
1. **Create simple spreadsheet** with the monthly tracker above 2. **Set monthly reminder** - first Friday of every month 3. **Pick your first action** - based on where you are now 4. **Start tracking this month** - even if numbers are small
**Remember:**
Small consistent improvements compound. Getting from $2K to $3K average client value = 50% revenue increase with same number of clients.
--- ← Previous [3 Steps to Maximise Your LTV](/resources/3-steps-to-maximise-your-ltv-24bf6d94771f80cba805d7f1d0cbe8ba) Next → [Build Your Revenue Ladder](/resources/build-your-revenue-ladder-24bf6d94771f800e86fed2264f6bcfed) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/scale-.content:
# What is a ‘Revenue Ladder’? # **What is a ‘Revenue Ladder’?** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** A revenue ladder is just a way to make more money from clients who already pay you. The aim of a revenue ladder is to maximise your LTV so you make more from your clients.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Understand what a revenue ladder actually is ✅ See why client ascension beats new client acquisition ✅ Know when to start building one ✅ Think about revenue growth the right way --- ## What Is A Revenue Ladder? --- **Simply:**
It's a system that gets you more money from the same clients.
**Instead of this:**
- Client pays $2K/month - Client churns - You hunt for next client
**You get this:**
- Client pays $2K/month for core service - Client pays $1K/month for add-on service - Client pays one-off $5K setup - **Total: $5K + $3K/month per client**
--- ## Why Client Ascension Beats New Clients --- **Example: Getting new clients is hard.**
- ~1,500 leads to get 1 client - 2-3 weeks to close - Lots of convincing and proof needed
**Selling to existing clients is easy:**
- They already trust you - They know your work quality - They don't need convincing you're legit - **One conversation = new revenue**
**Simple math:**
- New client acquisition: Weeks of work - Existing client upsell: One new offer + one call
--- ## Revenue Ladder Example (Lead Gen Agency) ---
**Client comes to you for lead generation.**
**Your ladder could look like:**
**Core Service:** Lead generation ($2K/month) **Add-On Service:** SDR calling service ($1.5K/month) **One-Off Setup:** Sales process optimization ($4K)
**Total first year:**
$4K + ($3.5K × 12) = **$46K per client vs. Core service only:** $2K × 12 = **$24K per client** **Extra revenue per client: $22K**
**The logic:**
- Leads need someone to call them (SDR service) increased TAM - More calls need better closing processes (sales optimisation) - Each step solves the problem your previous work creates
**Other ladder options based on your skills:**
- GTM strategy for new markets ($5K one-off) - Product-market fit research ($3K one-off) - Custom CRM setup + training ($3K one-off) - Sales frameworks and scripts ($1.5K one-off)
--- ## The Revenue Ladder Mindset Shift ---
**Old thinking:** "I need to find more clients."
**New thinking:** "I need to help my best clients more."
**This changes everything:**
- Your sales conversations get easier - Your marketing becomes referral-driven - Your revenue becomes more predictable - Your workload becomes more manageable
**Instead of chasing 15 new clients at $2K/month each:** **Help 8 existing clients at $3K+/month each.** Same revenue. Half the sales work. --- ## Start Building From Day One --- Don't wait until you're "ready." **Even at your first client, ask:**
- What could you add to your core service? - What do clients ask for after you deliver? - What problems does your work create?
**Simple test:**
After you deliver, ask: "What's your next biggest challenge?" Their answer = Your next service.
--- ← Previous [Build Your Revenue Ladder](/resources/build-your-revenue-ladder-24bf6d94771f800e86fed2264f6bcfed) Next → [3 Steps to Maximise Your LTV](/resources/3-steps-to-maximise-your-ltv-24bf6d94771f80cba805d7f1d0cbe8ba) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/scale-.title: Build Client Referral Program
closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/scale-.content:
# Build Client Referral Program # **Build Client Referral Program** ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Most agencies never ask for referrals systematically. The ones that do get 30%+ of new clients from referrals.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Understand what makes clients actually refer ✅ Know exactly when to ask for referrals ✅ Build 2 referral systems that work ✅ Create the right incentive structure --- ## What Makes Clients Refer --- **Clients refer when:**
- They've gotten clear, measurable results - The process was smooth (no drama or delays) - You solved a problem they couldn't solve themselves - They look good for making the introduction
**Clients DON'T refer when:**
- Results are unclear or hard to measure - The process was stressful or chaotic - You just delivered what they expected (no wow factor) - They're worried about looking bad if you mess up
**The key insight:**
Referrals happen when clients become advocates, not just satisfied customers.
--- ## When To Ask For Referrals --- **The perfect timing:**
Right after delivering a major win they can clearly see and measure.
**Specific timing examples:**
**Lead Gen Agency:** After 3 months of consistent lead delivery (not after first batch) **Web Design Agency:** 6 weeks after site launch when they see traffic/conversion improvements **Social Media Agency:** After hitting a major engagement milestone or follower target
**Never ask when:**
- You just finished onboarding - Results are mixed or unclear - They're dealing with other business problems - You haven't proven ROI yet
--- ## The 2 Referral Systems That Work --- ### 1. **The Results-Based Ask** **How it works:**
Ask immediately after sharing great results.
**The script:**
"Your lead generation hit 15 qualified meetings this month - that's 50% above target. I'm curious, do you know any other [industry] companies that might want similar results?"
**Why it works:**
- Timing is perfect (high on results) - Easy question to answer - Makes them think of specific people
**Follow-up:**
"Would you mind making a quick intro? I'll handle everything from there and make sure you look good for the referral."
### 2. **The Periodic Check-In System** **How it works:**
Build referral asks into your regular client updates.
**Monthly report email includes:**
- Results achieved this month - Key wins and improvements - Simple referral request: "Know anyone else who could use these results?"
**Quarterly business review includes:**
- 3-month results summary - ROI calculation - Referral conversation: "Based on these results, who else should we be talking to?"
**Why it works:**
- Regular touchpoints when results are fresh - Becomes part of normal business relationship - Multiple chances throughout the year
--- ## The Right Incentive Structure --- **For the client making the referral:** **Option 1:** Account credit
- $500 off next month's invoice per closed referral - Simple, immediate value
**Option 2:** Cash reward
- $500 cash per closed referral - Clean, no strings attached
**For the referred prospect: Option 3:** First month discount
- 50% off first month if they mention referrer - Easy sell, low risk for them to try
**What NOT to do:**
**Don't:** Offer percentage-based rewards (confusing) **Don't:** Make rewards too small ($50 gift cards) **Don't:** Require multiple referrals to qualify **Don't:** Make the reward process complicated
--- ## Your Referral System Setup --- **Step 1: Choose your ask timing**
**When will you ask for referrals?** - After what specific result: `_____________________` - How often in regular check-ins: `_____________________`
**Step 2: Write your referral script**
**Your results-based ask:** "Your [service] hit [specific result] - that's [% improvement]. I'm curious, do you know any other [target client type] that might want similar results?" **Fill in your version:** `_____________________`
**Step 3: Set your incentives**
**For referring clients:** `$_____ per closed referral`**For new prospects:** `_____% off first month`
--- ## Track Your Referral Success --- **Key metrics:**
- **Referral rate:** % of clients who make referrals - **Referral conversion:** % of referred prospects who close - **Referral revenue:** Monthly recurring from referrals - **Cost per referral:** Total incentives ÷ closed referrals
**Targets to aim for:**
- **25%+ referral rate** (1 in 4 clients makes referrals) - **40%+ referral conversion** (referrals close easier than cold prospects) - **30%+ of new clients** from referrals within 12 months
--- ## Common Referral Mistakes --- **Mistake 1:** Asking too early
**Wrong:** Ask during onboarding or after first delivery **Right:** Wait for clear, measurable results
**Mistake 2:** Being vague about who you want
**Wrong:** "Know anyone who needs marketing help?" **Right:** "Know any other SaaS companies with 20-100 employees?"
**Mistake 3:** Not following up on referrals
**Wrong:** Get introduced, then go silent **Right:** Update referring client on progress and outcomes
--- ← Previous [Client Upsells](/resources/client-upsells-24bf6d94771f8028ac79edf8162399f3) Next → [3 Steps To Maximize Your LTV](/resources/3-steps-to-maximise-your-ltv-24bf6d94771f80cba805d7f1d0cbe8ba) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/scale-.content:
# Client Upsells # Client Upsells ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Most agencies leave 50%+ of revenue on the table because they don't systematically upsell existing clients.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Know the 4 types of upsells that actually work ✅ Build your first upsell offer ✅ Learn when and how to present upsells ✅ Create a system to track upsell success --- ## The 4 Upsell Types That Work --- ### 1. **The Natural Next Step** **What it is:** Offer the logical next service after your core delivery. **Lead Gen Agency:**
- **Core:** Lead generation ($2K/month) - **Upsell:** Sales conversion training/closers ($1.5K/month) - **Logic:** "You're getting leads, now let's help you close more"
**Web Design Agency:**
- **Core:** Website build ($5K) - **Upsell:** Monthly maintenance ($500/month) - **Logic:** "Site's live, now let's keep it updated and secure"
**Why it works:**
- Solves the problem your core service creates - Natural progression in their mind - Easy to justify internally
--- ### 2. **The Expansion** **What it is:**
Add more channels or scale what you're already doing.
**Lead Gen Agency:**
- **Core:** Email lead generation ($2K/month) - **Upsell:** Add LinkedIn outreach ($1K/month extra) - **Logic:** "Email's working, let's add LinkedIn for 2x volume"
**Social Media Agency:**
- **Core:** Instagram management ($1.5K/month) - **Upsell:** Add TikTok + YouTube Shorts ($800/month) - **Logic:** "Instagram's growing, let's dominate all platforms"
**Why it works:**
- More of what's already working - Leverages existing success - Clear ROI they can see
--- ### 3. **The Premium Upgrade** **What it is:**
Offer a higher-tier version with additional value.
**Lead Gen Agency:**
- **Core:** Basic lead generation ($2K/month) - **Upsell:** Lead gen + SDR to maximise volume + sales coaching ($5.5K/month extra) - **Logic:** "You're getting meetings, let’s get you more and help you close more.”
**Web Design Agency:**
- **Core:** Basic website ($5K) - **Upsell:** Custom functionality + integrations ($3K extra) - **Logic:** "Site's working, now let's add advanced features"
**Why it works:**
- Higher value = higher price justified - Builds on existing relationship - Positions you as the complete solution
--- ### 4. **The Deal Structure Upgrade** **What it is:**
Restructure how they pay to get better rates + additional services.
**Lead Gen Agency (Pay-Per-Call to Retainer):**
- **Current:** $300/call × 10 calls = $3K/month - **Upsell:** $3.5K/month retainer = $270/call + call prep service - **Logic:** "Save $30/call and get prospect research documents + breakdown included for every call"
**Web Design Agency (Project to Retainer):**
- **Current:** $5K per website project - **Upsell:** $2K/month retainer = unlimited design requests - **Logic:** "Instead of one-off projects, get ongoing design support"
**Why it works:**
- They save money per unit - You get predictable monthly revenue - Natural way to add extra services - Easy internal sell ("we're saving money")
--- ## When To Present Your Upsell --- **The right time:** When they're getting results from your core service and asking "what's next?" **Timing examples:**
**Lead Gen:** After delivering consistent results for 3 months **Web Design:** 4 weeks after site launch when they see initial results **Social Media:** After second month when engagement/followers increase
**Warning signs to wait:**
- Core service isn't working yet - They're not happy with current results - They haven't paid last invoice on time
--- ## The Upsell Conversation Framework --- **Step 1: Highlight the win**
"Your lead generation is working great - you got 8 qualified leads this month, which is exactly what we targeted."
**Step 2: Identify the next bottleneck**
"Now that leads are flowing, what's happening when you get on the phone with them? Are you closing at the rate you want?"
**Step 3: Present the solution**
"This is exactly why we built our sales coaching service. Want me to show you how we've helped other clients close 40% more of these leads?"
**Step 4: Make it easy**
"It's $1.5K/month and we can start next week. Should we add it to your existing agreement?"
--- ## Build Your First Upsell --- **Use this framework:**
**Step 1:** What problem does your core service create or leave unsolved? **Step 2:** What do clients ask for after you deliver? **Step 3:** What related skill do you already have? **Step 4:** How can you package this into a monthly service?
**Example walkthrough:**
**Core service:** Email lead generation ($2K/month) **Problem it creates:** Clients get leads but struggle to close **What clients ask:** "Can you help us get ready to buy-leads?" **Your related skill:** You already qualify + generate the leads **Upsell package:** Ready-to-buy service (Extra $800/month) were you more thoroughly vet prospects only pushing through ones with budget (only an example. Don’t copy - think like a founder and build from your experiences.)
--- ## Track Your Upsell Success --- **Key metrics to watch:**
- **Upsell rate:** % of core clients who buy upsells - **Average time to upsell:** Days from core service start - **Upsell revenue:** Monthly recurring from upsells - **Total LTV:** Core + upsell revenue per client
**Targets to aim for:**
- **30%+ upsell rate** (3 out of 10 core clients buy upsells) - **60-90 days** average time to first upsell - **40%+ of revenue** from upsells within 12 months
--- ## Common Upsell Mistakes --- **Mistake 1:** Waiting too long to present
**Wrong:** Wait 6 months to mention upsells **Right:** Present within 30-60 days of good results
**Mistake 2:** Making it too complex
**Wrong:** "We offer 12 different add-on services..." **Right:** "Here's the one thing that makes sense next"
**Mistake 3:** Not connecting to current success**
**Wrong:** "Want to try our social media service?" **Right:** "Your leads are working great. Want to convert more of them?"
--- ← Previous [Front-End Offers](/resources/front-end-offers-24bf6d94771f80c19e3cc1bec8db3d1f) Next → [Build Client Referral Program](/resources/build-client-referral-program-24bf6d94771f8069816ce295ab6d5702) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/scale-.content:
# Front-End Offers # Front-End Offers ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** Front-end offers help you stand out when prospects are getting hit with identical pitches every week. It's about proving yourself first.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Understand what front-end offers are ✅ Know when to use them to cut through market noise ✅ See 3 types that build trust fast ✅ Pick the right one for your agency --- ## What Is A Front-End Offer? --- **Simply:**
It's a way to prove you can deliver before asking for a big commitment.
**Instead of this:**
- Prospect gets your $2K/month pitch - Sounds exactly like 5 other agencies this week - "We've been burned before" objection - No sale
**You get this:**
- Prospect sees your unique $1,000 sprint offer - You prove you can deliver in 30 days - They see results, trust builds - They upgrade to $2K/month - **Total: $1,000 + $2K/month per client**
--- ## When To Use Front-End Offers --- **You DON'T need front-end offers if:**
- Prospects readily buy your core service - You're closing 30%+ of qualified calls - Market isn't saturated with similar offers
**You DO need them if:**
- Prospects say "we've tried agencies before and got burned" - Your offer sounds like everyone else's - Prospects need to prove ROI internally first - Market is flooded with identical lead gen pitches
**Examples across different agencies:**
**Lead Gen:** Every agency offers "10 leads per month for $2K" **→ Sprint:** "5 qualified meetings in 30 days for $1,000"
**Web Design:** Everyone offers "custom website for $5K" **→ Sprint:** "Landing page conversion audit + 3 fixes for $500"
**Social Media:** All agencies pitch "full social management for $1.5K/month" **→ Sprint:** "30-day content strategy + first week's posts for $500"
--- ## 3 Types That Build Trust Fast --- ### 1. **The Sprint/Pilot** **What it is:**
Deliver a condensed version of your core service in 30 days.
**Lead Gen Agency:**
- **Offer:** "Lead Generation Sprint" ($1,000) - **Deliver:** 5 qualified meetings in 30 days - **Upsell:** "Now let's get you 15+ meetings per month"
**Web Design Agency:**
- **Offer:** "Conversion Audit + Quick Fixes" ($500) - **Deliver:** Hotjar analysis + 3 conversion improvements - **Upsell:** "Your conversions improved 40%. Want a full redesign?"
**Why it works:**
- Proves you can actually deliver - Addresses "we've been burned before" - Shows results fast
--- ### 2. **The Diagnostic/Audit** **What it is:**
Analyse their current approach and show exactly what's broken.
**Lead Gen Agency:**
- **Offer:** "Lead Generation Audit" ($500) - **Deliver:** Review current lead gen, find 3-5 gaps - **Upsell:** "We found why leads aren't converting. Want us to fix it?"
**Social Media Agency:**
- **Offer:** "Content Performance Audit" ($500) - **Deliver:** Analyse 90 days of content, identify top performers - **Upsell:** "Your best posts got 10x engagement. Want us to create more?"
**Why it works:**
- Uncovers problems they didn't know existed - Positions you as the expert - Creates urgency to fix issues
--- ### 3. **The Quick Win Setup** **What it is:**
Implement one piece of infrastructure that immediately improves their results.
**Lead Gen Agency:**
- **Offer:** "LinkedIn Lead Magnet Setup" ($500) - **Deliver:** Build lead capture system on their LinkedIn - **Upsell:** "Now let's fill this with 20+ leads per month"
**Web Design Agency:**
- **Offer:** "Website Speed Optimisation" ($1,000) - **Deliver:** Improve site speed by 50%+, install analytics - **Upsell:** "Speed improved, now let's optimise for conversions"
**Why it works:**
- Immediate improvement they can see - Shows you understand their business - Natural bridge to bigger engagement
--- ## Pick Your Front-End Offer --- **Use this framework:**
**Ask yourself:** 1. What objection do I hear most on sales calls? 2. How can I prove I can deliver in 30 days or less? 3. What one thing would immediately improve their situation?
--- ## Front-End Offer Rules --- **Rule 1:** Price it at 20-30% of your core service
- Core service: $2K/month - Front-end offer: $400-600
**Rule 2:** Deliver it in 30 days max
Fast results = fast trust building
**Rule 3:** Always auto-enroll into core service
"This sprint proves outbound works. If we deliver 5 qualified meetings, you automatically move to our monthly program."
**Rule 4:** Make it impossible to replicate
Don't give them a roadmap they can follow without you
--- ## Common Mistakes --- **Mistake 1:** Making it too complex
**Wrong:** "Complete lead generation overhaul with new website, CRM, and 12-email sequence" **Right:** "5 qualified meetings in 30 days"
**Mistake 2:** Not connecting to core service
**Wrong:** Social media audit → Lead gen service (disconnected) **Right:** Lead gen sprint → Lead gen retainer (natural progression)
**Mistake 3:** Giving away too much
**Wrong:** "Here's your complete lead gen strategy and email templates" **Right:** "Here are your 5 meetings. Want 15+ per month consistently?"
--- ## Your Front-End Offer Plan --- Fill this out: **My core service:** `_____________________` **Main objection I hear:** `_____________________` **What I can prove in 30 days:** `_____________________` **My front-end offer will be:** `_____________________` **Price:** `$_____` **Auto-enrollment condition:** `_____________________` --- ← Previous [3 Steps To Maximize Your LTV](/resources/3-steps-to-maximise-your-ltv-24bf6d94771f80cba805d7f1d0cbe8ba) Next → [Client Upsells](/resources/client-upsells-24bf6d94771f8028ac79edf8162399f3) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/scale-.content:
# How To Hire A VA # How To Hire A VA ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** You probably already have a VA, but if you don't, think like a founder. Hire for specific tasks, not a generic "assistant."
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Identify exactly which tasks you need help with ✅ Create a job post that attracts the right person ✅ Test candidates using your existing SOPs ✅ Hire someone who can actually follow instructions --- ## Think Tasks First, Not "VA" --- **Don't hire a "Virtual Assistant."** **Hire someone to do specific tasks you're currently doing.**
**Wrong approach:** "I need a VA to help with stuff" **Right approach:** "I need someone to handle client onboarding, update spreadsheets, and manage my calendar"
**List your tasks first:** --- **What tasks are eating your time?** 1. `_________________________________` 2. `_________________________________` 3. `_________________________________` 4. `_________________________________` 5. `_________________________________` **These tasks become your job posting.** --- ## Job Post Template --- **Use this template and fill in your specific tasks:**
**Job Title:** Virtual Assistant for [Your Agency Name] **About Us:** We're a [type] agency helping [target clients] with [your service]. We're looking for someone reliable to handle specific administrative tasks so our founder can focus on growth. **What You'll Do:** - [Task 1 from your list above] - [Task 2 from your list above] - [Task 3 from your list above] - [Task 4 from your list above] - [Task 5 from your list above] **Requirements:** - Fluent written English - Available [X] hours per week during [time zone] - Experience with [specific tools you use] - Reliable internet and workspace **To Apply:** Subject line: "VA Application - [Your Name]" Include: 1. Brief description of your relevant experience 2. Your hourly rate expectation 3. Your availability (hours/days) **Rate:** $5-10/hour depending on experience
**Post this on Upwork, OnlineJobs.ph, or other freelance platforms.** --- ## Interview Test Before You Hire --- **Don't hire based on their application alone.** **Test their ability to follow instructions using your SOPs.**
**Pick one SOP you've already stress-tested.** **Give them this as a paid test task ($5-20):** "Please complete this task using only the attached SOP. Do not ask questions. Let me know when it's complete."
### What You're Testing: --- ✅ **Can they follow written instructions?** ✅ **Do they ask unnecessary questions?** ✅ **Is the final result correct?** ✅ **How long did it take?** --- **If they pass the SOP test, they can probably handle the job.** **If they fail or ask lots of questions, your SOP needs work OR they're not the right person.** --- ## Red Flags During Testing --- **Don't hire if they:**
❌ Ask questions that are answered in the SOP ❌ Take 3x longer than expected ❌ Deliver incomplete or incorrect work ❌ Make excuses instead of following instructions
**You want someone who follows instructions precisely, not someone who thinks they know better.** --- ## Essential: Have SOPs for Everything --- **Before you delegate any task, make sure you have:**
✅ **A stress-tested SOP** for that task ✅ **Clear quality standards** they need to meet ✅ **Examples** of good vs. bad work ✅ **Tools and access** they need to complete it
**No SOP = Don't delegate yet.** **Build the SOP first, test it, then hand it off.** --- ← Previous [Who to Hire, When, and Why](/resources/who-to-hire-when-and-why-24bf6d94771f80afaad3f2a124d18c2e) Next → [How To Hire An Inbox Manager](/resources/how-to-hire-an-inbox-manager-24bf6d94771f80459fe1f5874ceb2fad) On this page

closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/scale-.title: How To Hire An Inbox Manager
closing-clients-30k-month-roadmap/scale-.content:
# How To Hire An Inbox Manager # How To Hire An Inbox Manager ---
**🚨 QUICK NOTE:** An inbox manager should respond to prospects in under 5 minutes while you're busy. This alone will book you 2-3 more calls per month.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- By the end of this page, you'll: ✅ Know exactly what an inbox manager does ✅ Get a proven job post template to find qualified candidates ✅ Learn how to test them before hiring ✅ See how to train them using our video training --- ## What an Inbox Manager Does --- **Simply:** They turn prospect replies into booked calls in under 5 minutes.
**Their main job:** - Monitor your/client inboxes for prospect replies - Respond within 5 minutes using your scripts - Book qualified sales calls - Handle follow-ups and scheduling
**Why this matters:** 5-minute response times get 100x higher connect rates than 30+ minute responses. --- ## Job Post Template ---
**Job Title:** Inbox Manager / Appointment Setter **About Us:** We're a [your service type] agency helping [your target clients] get more qualified sales calls. We need someone to manage prospect responses and book appointments. **What You'll Do:** - Monitor email inboxes for prospect replies - Respond to prospects within 5 minutes during business hours - Book qualified sales calls using our proven scripts - Follow up with prospects and manage scheduling **Requirements:** - Excellent written English (you'll be writing to business owners) - Experience with email support, chat support, or appointment setting - Available during US business hours [specify your timezone] - Reliable internet with backup plan for outages **To Apply:** Subject line: "Inbox Manager - [Your Name]" Include: 1. Your experience with email support or appointment setting 2. Your hourly rate expectation 3. Your availability in [your timezone] **Rate:** $6-12/hour depending on experience
**Post this on Upwork or OnlineJobs.ph** --- ## Test Before You Hire ---
**Give them this simple test ($10-20):** **Scenario:** "A prospect replied saying 'Can you send me more information about your pricing?'" **Instructions:** "Write exactly how you would reply to this prospect to book them a call. Keep it under 50 words. Do not ask me questions." **Look for:** - Do they avoid giving pricing over email? - Do they propose specific meeting times? - Is their English clear and professional? - Do they try to book a call instead of sending info?
**If they handle this correctly, they can do the job.** --- ## How to Train Your Inbox Manager --- **1. Watch this complete training video to see exactly how to set up and train your inbox manager:** [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qRRCdeo0jrg](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qRRCdeo0jrg)
**This video covers everything you need to successfully onboard and manage your inbox manager.**
--- **2. Go through our [Inbox Management](/resources/inbox-management-75e840029b8f41f092760aec9ccf6cc1) section** --- ## Essential Setup Before They Start --- **Before your inbox manager starts, make sure you have:**
✅ **Response templates** for common prospect replies ✅ **Client profiles** with booking instructions for each account ✅ **Slack or communication system** for instant notifications ✅ **Calendar access** so they can book calls directly ✅ **CRM/Pipeline Tracker access** to log replies/prospects
**No setup = confused inbox manager = poor results.** --- ← Previous [How To Hire A VA](/resources/how-to-hire-a-va-24bf6d94771f80db882efcb7a86a80a0) Next → [How To Hire An SDR](/resources/how-to-hire-an-sdr-24bf6d94771f809bbc18f1d532fd42fe) On this page

# Cold Calling Tech Stack # Cold Calling Tech Stack ---
📌 **The Closing Clients Recommended Cold Calling Tech Stack** This stack lets you run high-volume cold calling at 1/10th the cost of traditional SDRs. Multiple CCG members are booking 15-30 meetings/month with this exact setup.
## What This Page Will Help You Do --- ✅ Understand what each tool does and why it matters ✅ Choose the right setup for your situation (DIY, outsourced, or hybrid) ✅ Budget properly with realistic cost breakdowns --- ## The Stack Overview --- | Tool | What it solves | | --- | --- | | **No Accent Callers (NAC)** | "I can't afford SDRs" - offshore team at 1/10th the cost | | **LeadMagic** | "I can't find mobile numbers" - pay only for numbers found | | **Salesfinity** | "I waste time on voicemails" - AI dialer skips to live conversations | --- ## No Accent Callers (NAC) ---
🔗 [**noaccentcallers.com**](http://noaccentcallers.com)
### What Is It? --- NAC is an offshore cold calling team based in Spain. You give them your ICP and they handle everything: list building, dialing, and booking appointments directly into your calendar. Think of it as an SDR team you can turn on without the $60K/year salary. ### Why It Works --- The callers are accent-neutral. That matters because prospects don't mentally check out the second they hear an offshore accent. They sound like they could be calling from your office. ### What You Get ---
- Full cold calling service (they do the calls, not you) - List building included - Appointments booked directly to your calendar - 80% cheaper than US/UK SDRs
### What It Costs --- Pricing is custom based on volume. Expect $400-900/month for 2,000-3,000 calls. ### Who This Is For --- Agency owners who want cold calling results without doing the calling themselves. If you'd rather show up to booked meetings than grind the phones, this is your move. --- ## LeadMagic ---
🔗 [**leadmagic.io**](http://leadmagic.io)
### What Is It? --- LeadMagic finds mobile phone numbers for your leads. Upload a list of names and companies, get back direct mobile numbers. The key difference from Apollo or ZoomInfo: you only pay for numbers they actually find. No wasted credits on "mobile not available." ### Why It Works --- Most B2B databases give you office numbers. Office numbers go to gatekeepers. Mobile numbers go directly to decision makers. LeadMagic's data is also fresher. They validate in real-time instead of serving you 2-year-old records. ### How It Works ---
1. Upload your lead list (names + companies) 2. LeadMagic searches for mobile numbers 3. Download enriched list 4. Pay only for the numbers found
### What It Costs --- Starts at $75/month for 100 leads. Pay-as-you-go after that. For teams doing under 5K leads/month, it's 50-70% cheaper than Apollo's phone data. They give you 100 free credits to test. ### Who This Is For --- Anyone doing cold calling who's tired of bad phone data. If you're currently using Apollo for mobiles and getting 40%+ "not found," switch to this. --- ## Salesfinity ---
🔗 [**salesfinity.co**](http://salesfinity.co)
### What Is It? --- Salesfinity is an AI parallel dialer. It calls multiple prospects at the same time and only connects you when a real human picks up. No more listening to 47 voicemails in a row. ### Why It Works --- Normal dialing: call, voicemail, hang up, call, voicemail, hang up. You spend 80% of your time NOT talking to anyone. Parallel dialing: the system calls 5 people at once. The moment someone answers, you're connected. Everyone else gets dropped. **The numbers:** 5.2x more live conversations than normal dialers. 100 calls in 27 minutes. 5-10 actual conversations. ### What You Get ---
- AI parallel dialing (calls multiple lines simultaneously) - Automatic voicemail detection and skip - Caller ID rotation (so you don't show up as spam) - Prospect intel shown before call connects - Real-time objection handling suggestions - Integrates with Salesforce, HubSpot, Outreach, Salesloft, Apollo
### What It Costs --- Silver Plan starts around $149-299/user/month. Gold Plan runs $349/user/month with more features. Enterprise is custom. No free trial available. ### Who This Is For --- People who want to do the calling themselves but hate wasting time on voicemails. If you've got 2 hours a day for calls and want to maximize live conversations, this is it. --- ## How To Use This Stack ---
1️⃣ **Option 1: Do It Yourself** Build your list. Enrich with LeadMagic. Load into Salesfinity. Make the calls yourself. **Best for:** Founders who want direct prospect feedback and don't mind being on the phones.
2️⃣ **Option 2: Fully Outsourced** Send your ICP to NAC. They build the list, make the calls, book the meetings. You just show up. **Best for:** Agency owners who want meetings without the grind. Higher cost, zero time investment.
3️⃣ **Option 3: Hybrid** Use LeadMagic to enrich your list with mobile numbers. Send the enriched list to NAC for calling. **Best for:** When you want control over list quality but don't want to dial. Best of both worlds.
--- ## Cost Comparison --- | Approach | Monthly Cost | Calls/Month | Your Time | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | US-based SDR | $4,000-6,000 | 2,000-3,000 | Management overhead | | NAC only | $400-900 | 2,000-3,000 | None | | Salesfinity + your time | $149-349 | 3,000-5,000 | 2+ hours/day | | Full stack | $600-1,200 | 5,000-10,000 | Minimal | These are estimates. Your actual costs depend on volume and how you structure things. ← Previous [Cold Calling](/resources/cold-calling-2edf6d94771f8199aacff6b2ea75c564) Next → [Scripts & Templates](/resources/scripts-templates-2edf6d94771f81a6b853c0bda530e96b) On this page

# Should I Add Cold Calling? # Should I Add Cold Calling? ---
🚨 **Before You Dive Into The Tech Stack** Cold calling isn't for everyone. This page helps you figure out if it makes sense for YOUR situation right now.
## Two Reasons To Add Cold Calling ---
1️⃣ **You're Just Starting Out (More Time Than Money)** If you don't have clients yet and you're bootstrapping, cold calling is one of the best revenue-generating activities you can do. No software costs. No waiting 2 weeks for email warmup. No deliverability issues. Just pick up the phone and talk to people. Do it in your spare time. Every call is a chance to book a meeting. **Best for:** People starting from zero who want to generate revenue NOW.
2️⃣ **You Have Clients And Want To Scale (Supplement Cold Email)** Once you've got revenue coming in, cold calling becomes a way to expand your reach. Here's the thing most people don't realise: **Cold email only reaches about 40% of your total addressable market.** The other 60%? You can't find valid emails for them. Those leads just sit in your list doing nothing. Cold calling unlocks that 60%. You're not replacing email. You're reaching the people email can't. **Best for:** Agencies doing cold email who want to expand TAM and stop leaving leads on the table.
## The 60% Problem --- ### What's Happening Right Now --- When you build a lead list, you'll typically find valid emails for about 40% of them. The rest either:
- Have no email on file - Have outdated/invalid emails - Use catch-all domains you can't verify
Those leads get thrown away. You spent time finding them and they go nowhere. ### What Cold Calling Fixes --- That 60% of leads you couldn't email? You can call them. Meaning you can instantly DOUBLE your TAM (total addressable market) --- ## When To Add Cold Calling --- | Your Situation | When To Start | How To Do It | | --- | --- | --- | | Just starting, no clients | Now | DIY with Salesfinity or just your phone | | Have clients, limited budget | When you can afford $400-600/mo | Outsource to NAC | | Scaling, want full control | When you can afford $1,500-2,000/mo | Hire your own SDR | --- ## Ready To Get Started? --- If cold calling makes sense for you, here's where to go next:
👉 [**Cold Calling Tech Stack →**](/resources/cold-calling-tech-stack-2edf6d94771f8146906cc36c907e08c5) The tools to run cold calling at 1/10th the cost. Outsource or DIY.

Each campaign can be a different angle or target and you can have A/B tests within each campaign ## Step 3: Use 6 Domains Per Campaign We recommend 6 domains per campaign. This is recommended based on our [Cold Email Set Up](/resources/cold-email-set-up-139f6d94771f811f8e77fa8c4669138a) ## Step 4: Create a Three Email Campaign Sequence We recommend having a 3 email campaign sequence. Meaning, 1 initial email and 2 follow-up emails ## Step 5: Sending schedule | **Setting** | **Recommendation** | | --- | --- | | **Time-Zone** | Use the time-zone that applies to your lead lists | | **Campaign Hours** | 08:00am to 7:00pm | | **Days To Send** | Weekdays ONLY | ## Step 6: Recommended Campaign Settings Checklist - [ ] Send Emails as PLAIN TEXT ONLY - [ ] Auto Stop Sending Emails On Reply - [ ] Disable Open Tracking - [ ] Disable Link Tracking - [ ] Make Gap Between Emails Minimum 5-Minutes - [ ] Prioritise Reaching Out To New Leads Over Follow-Ups - [ ] Auto optimise A/Z testing and Chose Reply Rate As Winning Metric - [ ] Enable Provider Matching # Recommended Sending Limits
\nℹ️ **For all current warmup and sending settings, see:**\n\n[**Recommended Settings →**](/resources/key-resources/recommended-settings)\n\n
\n\n**1. We recommend sending 25 emails per day, per inbox** ![Screenshot 2024-08-13 at 10.56.26.png](Campaign%20Set%20Up/Screenshot_2024-08-13_at_10.56.26.png) **2. Run warmup for 14 days at a 2 email ramp up** ![Screenshot 2024-07-20 at 9.38.58 AM.png](Campaign%20Set%20Up/Screenshot_2024-07-20_at_9.38.58_AM.png) **3. Start sending at 5 emails per day per inbox and ramp up to 25 per day over the next 14 days. This is the ramp up schedule we would recommend (weekends included)** - *Days 1 - 3:* **5 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 4 - 6:* **10 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 6 - 9:* **15 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 10 - 12:* **20 emails a da**y **per inbox** - *Days 13+:* **25 emails a day per inbox** # Campaign Settings Calculator We have created a campaigns settings calculator to help with sending pattern settings within campaigns. Sending patter settings are when you’re asked to input: 1. Daily Limit 2. Time-Gap Between Emails 3. Max New Leads Per Day **CLICK HERE TO ACCESS THE CALCULATOR👇** [Campaign Settings Calculator](https://www.closingclientsgroup.com/campaign-settings)
💡 Remember to ramp up your campaigns using the schedule below. You can do this by increasing the number of new leads so the right number of emails go out each day per inbox depending on the number of inboxes in the campaign - *Days 1 - 3:* **5 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 4 - 6:* **10 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 6 - 9:* **15 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 10 - 12:* **20 emails a da**y **per inbox** - *Days 13+:* **25 emails a day per inbox**

# Deliverability Updates: What's Working NOW ---
ℹ️ **This page is updated monthly with critical changes to cold email deliverability and infrastructure.** **Last Updated:** December 28, 2025 Check this page at the start of each month to stay ahead of deliverability changes.
--- ## 🚨 Critical Updates ---
ℹ️ If this section is blank then you don’t have to worry. However if there are updates, consider making changes immediately.
### Critical Update #1 - Copy fingerprinting has accelerated (again) ---
**What changed** - Google + Microsoft are flagging repeated phrasing **faster than October** - Spintax permutations are being fingerprinted at the *phrase level* - Entire sequences can be poisoned by **one flagged sentence** (often CTA, signature, job title, or phone number) **Observed effects** - Campaigns dropping from ~1.5%+ to **0.4–0.7%** within 2–3 weeks - “Burned infra” often turns out to be **burned copy** **Recommendations** - Reduce spintax complexity (3 variants > 50 permutations) - Rotate *structures*, not synonyms (words) - Test copy every **week**
--- ### **Critical Update #2 - Outlook filtering behavior has changed** ---
**What changed** - Outlook appears to apply **post-delivery LLM review** - Emails can land in inbox, then move to spam 30–60s later - Many placement tests are now **false positives** **Key insight** > Outlook placement tests ≠ real inboxing > Bot-based tests often open emails → send positive signals → skew results. **Recommendations** - Use **real, aged human Outlook accounts** as placement tests (old Hotmail / Outlook accounts used for years) - Ignore “green” placement tests unless verified by real inboxes - Focus on **human replies only**
--- ## 📢  Non-Critical Updates ---
ℹ️ Non-Critical updates based on what we’re seeing working best right now.
### **Update #1 - Outlook vs Google is unstable**
**Outlook Inboxes:** Although over the past 15 weeks, we’re consistently seeing Outlook inboxes outperform Google. We still recommend a 50/50 allocation. **Recommendations:** - Make sure to utilise your Outlook inboxes and/or purchase some if you don’t have any - Keep infra 50/50 between Google and Outlook (Azure included)
### Signal #2 - Volume sensitivity is increasing
**Observed** - Lower volume per inbox is outperforming: – 2–10 emails/day > 15–30/day - Outlook especially punishes burst sends **Recommendations** - Cap campaigns at low daily limits - Stagger send times across multiple campaigns - Avoid backlog “catch-up” bursts from sequencers
## 🔍 What We're Currently Reviewing ---
**We're actively testing and researching:** - Premium warmup pools vs built-in warmup - 30-day warmup vs 14-day warmup - Aged domain setups vs new domains Results will be published here once validated.
