# Deliverability - Control panel ⚡️ [Template page](/resources/template-page-142f6d94771f81648f4ece5194213592) # Deliverability --- # 1. Follow Our Recommendations
\nℹ️ **For all warmup, sending, and ramp-up settings, see:**\n\n[**Recommended Settings →**](/resources/key-resources/recommended-settings)\n\n
\n\n## CCG Recommendations 1. **Buy 50% Google and 50% Outlook domains** 2. **Use 1 inbox per domain** 3. **Set up inboxes using Incogniton** 4. **Use master inboxes, not Unibox etc** 5. **Warm new domains for 4 days only** 6. **Send 25 emails/day per inbox**
💡 Remember to ramp up your volume using the schedule below. You can do this by increasing the number of new leads so the right number of emails go out each day per inbox depending on the number of inboxes in the campaign - *Days 1 - 3:* **5 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 4 - 6:* **10 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 6 - 9:* **15 emails a day per inbox** - *Days 10 - 12:* **20 emails a da**y **per inbox** - *Days 13+:* **25 emails a day per inbox**
7. **Send emails as plain-text (do not track opens)** 8. **Test inboxes weekly** ## General Best Practices We understand that some may be hesitant to run outbound campaigns without warming up. Here are the general best practices we recommend, should you decide not to follow our guidance: 1. **Buy 50% Google and 50% Outlook domains** 2. **1 or 2 inboxes per domain** 3. **Warm new domains for 14-21 days**
💡 Warmup Settings: - Warmup per day: 40 - Ramp up: 5 per day - Randomise: 15-40 - Reply Rate: 35%
1. **Send max 20 cold emails per day per account**
💡 Warmup While Sending (2x Rule): - Warmup per day: 2x your cold email volume (e.g., 40 if sending 20 cold emails) - Randomise: 20-40 - Reply Rate: 20% **For all current settings, see [Recommended Settings](/resources/key-resources/recommended-settings)**
1. **Send emails as plain-text (do not track opens)** 2. **Test inboxes weekly** # 2. Deliverability Check List **Running Outreach Checklist:** - [ ] **Bounce Rate Management:** Keeping bounce rate BELOW 2% - [ ] **Time-to-lead:** Respond to leads within 5-minutes - [ ] **Plain Text Emails**: Send emails in plain text to improve deliverability - [ ] **Domain and Inbox Setup**: Confirm that domains and inboxes are configured according to our resources - [ ] **Targeted Sending**: Make sure you’re sending emails to well-researched and segmented audience to increase relevance and response rates - [ ] **Domain Health Check**: Regularly check that none of your domains are blacklisted to avoid deliverability issues - [ ] **Inbox Placement Score**: Monitor your inbox placement score to ensure your emails are reaching the intended inbox - [ ] **Time-Zone Awareness**: Send your emails according to the recipient's time zone for higher engagement - [ ] **Optimal Sending Times**: Make sure you’re sending at the best times to increase open rates - [ ] **Lead Segmentation**: Segment your leads for more personalised and targeted outreach - [ ] **Subject Line Strategy**: Mimic internal emails to increase open rates - [ ] **Email Length**: Keep your emails concise, aiming for under 250 words - [ ] **Clear Call to Action**: Include one clear call to action in every email. Don’t overwhelm them - [ ] **Follow-Up:** Follow up! - [ ] **Email Testing**: Test send your emails to check their appearance on both desktop and mobile. - [ ] **Test Email Angles:** Use A/B tests to test variations - [ ] **Preview Text Review**: Optimise the preview text of your emails 3. General Best Practices Following these fundamental will help improve your deliverability: ### 1. Subject Lines **Avoid Over-Promises:** Keep subject lines realistic to avoid triggering skepticism and spam filters **Examples to Avoid:** - "Earn $10,000 working from home" suggests improbable earnings - "URGENT: Limited Time Offer!" uses unnecessary urgency and capitalisation ### **2. Spam Triggers** Certain words are more likely to mark your email as spam. Whilst context matters, use these words carefully - **Common Triggers:** "Free," "Guarantee," "No risk," and "Urgent."
💡 Use MailMeteor to check your copy for spam words: [MailMeteor](https://mailmeteor.com/spam-checker)
### **3. Links and Images** - **Limit Links:** Avoid using links if you can. If you must, only use ONE link and have that link be your website domain link or a link that relates to your domain (not a 3rd part link) - **Image Use:** Avoid images - **Send emails as plain text** - **Unsubscribe Link:** Always include an option to unsubscribe. You can also use a text-based opt out to avoid sending links ### **4. Industry-Specific Keywords** Different industries have specific trigger words. Knowing them helps tailor your content for better inbox placement. - **Finance:** Avoid aggressive financial promises like "Quick cash" or "No credit check." - **Recruitment:** Terms like "Extra income" or "Work from home" can be sensitive. - **Marketing:** Be cautious with "Bulk email," "Increase sales," or "Click here." - **Health:** Claims like "Miracle cure" or "Fast weight loss" are red flags ### 5. Spintax Make sure you are using spintax when writing your emails to further avoid landing in spam **👇 Click here to access our resource on Spintax** [Writing Emails](/resources/writing-emails-9bc75337ac584d679d465712848be02c) ### 6. Guide To Managing Deliverability Main points to consider in ensuring good deliverability [https://www.loom.com/share/6dea37f79a3d451dab9cb19f2d1a066f](https://www.loom.com/share/6dea37f79a3d451dab9cb19f2d1a066f) # **3. Testing & Managing Email Deliverability** Managing email deliverability is SUPER important. You can have the most targeted leads and best written emails in the world but if your deliverability is off, it doesn’t mean anything
⚠️ **We recommend that you run weekly deliverability tests for ALL domains**

Troubleshooting Deliverability Issues** ⚠️ **When to Follow This Process** 1. **Inbox Score:** 8.4/10 or below. 2. **Blacklisting:** If your inbox is blacklisted on any site except SORBS. ## **Step-by-Step Process** 1. **Remove Affected Inboxes:** Immediately remove these inboxes from your campaigns. 2. **Diagnose Issues:** Use tools like Mail-Tester to identify problems (e.g., DNS issues). 3. **Blacklisted?** - Stop sending from blacklisted inboxes. - Wait 3 days, then retest. - If still blacklisted, wait an additional 4 days and retest. - Consider using this Fiverr service to remove the blacklist if still persists after 7 days
🔗 [Delist Your IP From Blacklist](https://www.fiverr.com/ritesh_so/delist-your-ip-from-blacklist-and-improve-email-delivery?source=order_page_summary_gig_link_title&funnel=092731da34b2b5fc91c46cca2518e4e2)
4. **Resume Sending:** Once the score improves to 8.5/10 or above, or the blacklist is removed, add the inboxes back to your campaigns. # 5. Other Deliverability Tools If you don’t want to use the tools we’ve recommended, here’s some others you could consider: - **EmailGuard** ---
